allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: codegraph
allTools.description: Initialize, search, or explore the CodeGraph index for the current Pi workspace only. This tool never accepts a project path or shell command.
promptGuidelines: Use operation init before querying when the current workspace has no .codegraph index.
Use query for symbol search and explore for source plus call paths. Do not use this tool to run arbitrary commands or target another directory.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: gentle_review
allTools.description: Inspect and recover review authority, run new native ordinary review through start/finalize/validate, preserve legacy compact compatibility reads and graph-v1 Judgment Day, and authorize one exact lifecycle command. FINALIZE input is a JSON string: review_result.lens_results[] entries contain lens, findings, and non-empty evidence exactly once for every lens selected by START; final_evidence and final_verification_passed are paired. This is the Pi wrapper contract, distinct from native CLI --result, --refuter, --validation, and --evidence files. RESET/RECOVER remain destructive and are executed by the audited native CLI: RESET and RECOVER_LOCK map to `gentle-ai review reclaim` and RECOVER maps to `gentle-ai review recover` with the provider-selected disposition. Published v2.1.11 repair-legacy-alias derives its fixed repository binding from fresh native inventory before fresh UI approval; dispose-result remains unsupported pending design. Legacy bundle transport is retired: export/import return a legacy-operation-retired envelope pointing at the native gentle-ai review CLI and the Git common-directory store.

changeName.description: Canonical OpenSpec change name required to resolve a recovered authority during lifecycle validate.

command.description: One exact direct lifecycle command for validate.

idempotencyKey.description: Required for graph-v1 start/advance and lifecycle validate operations.

input.description: A JSON-serialized object string, not a nested object. New native ordinary START uses {"mode":"ordinary"}; an explicit baseRef requires committedOnly: true and requests a committed range, while repository-local policyPath remains optional. Legacy compact START retains policyHash. FINALIZE supplies reviewer results, correction forecast, targeted validation, final evidence, and an explicit final_verification_passed boolean. Judgment Day retains graph-v1 input.

inputPath.description: Repository-local JSON input file for finalize/advance (alternative to input). Legacy bundle import is retired.

lineageId.description: Bounded review lineage identifier. A failed start creates no lineage; do not use it with status or advance.

lineageIds.description: Retired with legacy bundle export; ignored. Export returns legacy-operation-retired.

operation.description: Controller operation. Inspect authority before start. Reset requires the exact challenge returned by inspect.

operationId.description: Retired with legacy bundle transport; ignored. Export/import return legacy-operation-retired.

outputPath.description: Retired with legacy bundle export; ignored. Export returns legacy-operation-retired.

transition.description: A supported REVIEW_TRANSITION value for advance.

workspaceRoot.description: Optional absolute Git worktree root that owns this review (for example the SDD apply worktree). It must be an existing worktree root sharing the session repository's Git common directory; validation fails closed otherwise. Absent, the session cwd is used unchanged.

promptGuidelines: Call {"operation":"inspect"} before START. New native ordinary START uses a JSON string such as "{\"mode\":\"ordinary\"}"; an explicit baseRef must be paired with committedOnly: true to request a committed range, while policyPath remains repository-local. policyHash is legacy compact-only. The controller derives lineage, Git/untracked scope, tier, lenses, authored lines, and budget.

Use RECONCILE_AUTHORITY only to quarantine one invalid native recovery successor. Supply exact predecessorLineage, expectedPredecessorRevision, successorLineage, expectedSuccessorRevision, actor, and reason values; Pi derives and displays the seven-line native authorization binding for fresh UI approval. The predecessor stays untouched, native returns the durable audit record, and Pi never falls back to RESET or RECOVER.

Use ABANDON or QUARANTINE_LEGACY only after an explicit user decision and with exact native inputs. ABANDON needs lineage, expectedRevision, snapshotIdentity, actor, and reason; QUARANTINE_LEGACY accepts only the published malformed freeze-findings diagnostic/disposition. A dual reconciliation may supply only anomalies `unchanged_target,malformed_recovery_authorization` in that exact order. Use REPAIR_LEGACY_ALIAS only with lineage, actor, and reason: Pi freshly reads native inventory and derives repository, revision, diagnostic, disposition, and the exact eight-line binding before interactive approval. `review dispose-result` is unsupported pending design.

Run selected lenses once, then call FINALIZE with a JSON string containing review_result.lens_results entries for every START-selected lens. Each entry has lens, findings, and non-empty evidence; clean lenses use findings: []. Pair final_evidence with final_verification_passed. This Pi wrapper shape differs from native CLI --result, --refuter, --validation, and --evidence files. FINALIZE alone records correction forecast, Git-derived correction evidence, targeted validation, and final evidence. Use ADVANCE only for explicit graph-v1 Judgment Day.

For blocked-legacy or blocked-mixed, do not call START repeatedly. Explain invalidation, request explicit user authorization for the exact reset_request challenge, then call RESET or RECOVER only after authorization. RESET and RECOVER_LOCK route to audited native `gentle-ai review reclaim` and RECOVER routes to native `gentle-ai review recover`; negotiated target status supplies the sole accepted recovery disposition, and a caller-supplied substitute is rejected. Treat a native-input-required envelope as a request for exact values, never as permission to invent them. After a committed native recovery record, INSPECT before any fresh ordinary START.

A reported lineage_created false or pre-authority validation error proves no lineage was created. After ambiguous START or FINALIZE output, the controller calls target-scoped native status first and returns only its declared action. Never infer or prescribe replay unless native explicitly reports exact_replay_safe for the same canonical request and required lineage.
Use gentle_review for bounded review transaction operations and exact lifecycle validation; never fabricate bash tool metadata or a separate gate target.

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: multi_grep
allTools.description: Search file contents using multiple patterns (OR logic)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

added.name: agent_browser
added.description: Browse and interact with websites using agent-browser. Use this for web research, reading live docs, opening pages, taking snapshots or screenshots, clicking links, filling forms, extracting page content, and authenticated/profile-based browser work. Input choice: default `args` for open → snapshot -i → click/fill @refs; `semanticAction` for stable role/text/label targets; `job` or `qa` for multi-step checks; `electron` only for desktop apps; experimental `sourceLookup` / `networkSourceLookup` for candidates only.

args.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name and any shell operators. Required unless semanticAction, job, qa, sourceLookup, networkSourceLookup, or electron is provided. Do not include --json (wrapper injects it). Typical first calls: open, snapshot -i, click/fill current @refs, then snapshot -i again after navigation or DOM changes.

items.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name. Do not pass --json; the wrapper injects it. First-call recipe: open → snapshot -i → click/fill @eN → snapshot -i.

action.description: List discovered Electron apps.

maxResults.description: Maximum electron.list apps to return. Defaults to 50; values above 200 are clamped.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter for electron.list across app name, bundle id, desktop id, and paths.

action.description: Launch an Electron app with an isolated wrapper-owned profile.

allow.description: Optional caller-owned allow list for electron.launch policy checks.

items.description: App identifier allowed by the caller for electron.launch.

appArgs.description: Optional Electron app argv. Wrapper-owned lifecycle/debug flags are rejected.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

appPath.description: Electron launch target: macOS .app bundle path. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

targetType.description: Preferred CDP target type. Defaults to page.

action.description: Launch an Electron app with an isolated wrapper-owned profile.

allow.description: Optional caller-owned allow list for electron.launch policy checks.

items.description: App identifier allowed by the caller for electron.launch.

appArgs.description: Optional Electron app argv. Wrapper-owned lifecycle/debug flags are rejected.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

appName.description: Electron launch target: app display name discovered by electron.list. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

targetType.description: Preferred CDP target type. Defaults to page.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

bundleId.description: Electron launch target: macOS bundle identifier discovered by electron.list. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

allow.description: Optional caller-owned allow list for electron.launch policy checks.

items.description: App identifier allowed by the caller for electron.launch.

appArgs.description: Optional Electron app argv. Wrapper-owned lifecycle/debug flags are rejected.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

executablePath.description: Electron launch target: executable path. Discovery is not required when this is provided. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

targetType.description: Preferred CDP target type. Defaults to page.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup one wrapper-tracked Electron launch by launchId.

launchId.description: Wrapper launch id for electron.status and electron.cleanup.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup all wrapper-tracked Electron launches.

all.description: Apply electron.status or electron.cleanup to all wrapper-owned launches.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup the only active wrapper-tracked Electron launch.
Probe the current attached Electron managed session; launchId is accepted for launch-scoped follow-up actions.

launchId.description: Wrapper launch id for electron.probe follow-up targeting.

electron.description: Electron wrapper action. Fields are action-specific and unsupported fields are rejected.

failFast.description: Stop the compiled batch on the first failed job step. Defaults to true so later mutating steps do not run after setup/assertion failures.

action.description: Constrained one-call job step compiled to existing upstream batch commands.

delayMs.description: Optional per-character delay for type steps; when set, the job compiles to focus/keyboard type/wait steps instead of instant fill-like typing, capped at 200 characters.

loadState.description: Optional readiness wait to insert immediately after an open step; use domcontentloaded/load/networkidle when the next job step needs page hydration evidence before clicking or reading.

locator.description: Semantic locator for click/fill steps when selector is omitted.

name.description: Accessible name filter for role locator click/fill steps.

path.description: Artifact/download path for waitForDownload or screenshot steps.

press.description: Optional key to press after a type step, for example Enter.

role.description: Role locator value for click/fill steps when locator is role.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for click/fill/type/select-like steps; omit when using semantic locator fields on click/fill steps.

text.description: Text for fill steps or visible text for assertText steps.

url.description: URL for open steps; exact URL or * / ** glob-style URL pattern for assertUrl steps.

value.description: Single option value for select steps, or locator value for semantic click/fill steps.

values.description: One or more option values for select steps.

items.description: Option value for select steps.

networkSourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: failed-request-to-source candidates only (initiator metadata and bounded workspace URL literals; not definitive blame). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

filter.description: Optional upstream network requests filter pattern.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan for URL literals. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

namespace.description: Optional upstream namespace; prepends --namespace <name> before the generated batch.

requestId.description: Optional network request id to inspect with network request <id>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the generated batch.

url.description: Optional failed request URL or URL fragment to correlate with local source.

outputPath.description: Optional workspace-relative or absolute file path that receives the model-facing command data/result after the browser command completes. Useful for eval/get/snapshot captures that should become durable local artifacts.

attached.description: Run the QA preset against the currently attached session instead of opening qa.url.

checkConsole.description: Whether to inspect console messages and fail on console errors. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkErrors.description: Whether to inspect page errors and fail when errors are present. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

attached.description: When omitted or false, qa.url is required and opened before checks.

checkConsole.description: Whether to fail on console error messages. Defaults to true.

checkErrors.description: Whether to fail on page errors. Defaults to true.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to true.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

url.description: URL to open for a lightweight QA preset.

qa.description: Lightweight QA preset. Use qa.url to open a URL, or qa.attached=true to check the current attached session without opening a URL.

action.description: Intent action to compile to an existing agent-browser find command, direct selector/ref command, or upstream select when action=select.

locator.description: Upstream find locator family to use for check/click/fill actions.

name.description: Accessible name filter for locator=role; compiles to --name <name>.

role.description: Role locator value for locator=role. May be used instead of value; when both are set they must match.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for direct click/check/fill actions, or for select actions compiled to select <selector> <value...>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the compiled command.

value.description: Locator value for find actions, or a single option value for select actions. For locator=role, role may be supplied instead.

values.description: One or more option values for select actions.

items.description: Option value for select actions.

sessionMode.description: Session handling mode. `auto` reuses the extension-managed pi-scoped session when possible. `fresh` switches that managed session to a fresh upstream launch so launch-scoped flags like --namespace, --restore, --restore-save, restore check flags, --profile, --executable-path, --webgpu, --session-name, --cdp, --state, --auto-connect, --init-script, --enable, -p/--provider, or iOS --device apply and later auto calls follow the new browser.

sourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: local UI-to-source candidates only (confidence/evidence, not guaranteed mappings). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

componentName.description: Component name to correlate with react tree output and bounded local workspace search.

includeDomHints.description: Whether selector lookups should inspect DOM HTML attributes for source-like metadata. Defaults to true.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan when componentName is provided. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

reactFiberId.description: React fiber id to inspect with upstream react inspect. Requires a session opened with --enable react-devtools.

selector.description: Visible selector or @ref whose DOM metadata should be inspected for source hints.

stdin.description: Optional raw stdin content; only supported for batch, eval --stdin, auth save --password-stdin, and is generated internally by job, qa, sourceLookup, or networkSourceLookup mode. Do not use with electron mode.

timeoutMs.description: Optional per-call wrapper subprocess watchdog in milliseconds for browser CLI args/job/qa/source lookup calls. Use for long opens or large output captures; explicit long wait steps are forwarded, so set timeoutMs above the wait duration plus a small grace window when overriding the derived watchdog. Electron actions use electron.timeoutMs instead.

promptGuidelines: Use agent_browser for real browser or live web content.

For detailed agent_browser docs, read targeted sections: /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/README.md (setup), /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/docs/COMMAND_REFERENCE.md (commands), /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/docs/TOOL_CONTRACT.md (result/details). Do not load the full command reference unless needed.

Use agent_browser with one input mode: args, semanticAction, job, qa, sourceLookup/networkSourceLookup, or electron. stdin only for batch/eval/auth/wrapper batch; electron rejects stdin; never pass --json.

For agent_browser, use open → snapshot -i → current @refs or semanticAction → re-snapshot after navigation/scroll/rerender. Batch same-snapshot forms; split before navigation/submits. Stop before order/post/purchase/submit.

Use agent_browser sessionMode=fresh for launch-scoped flags, including --allowed-domains; never put --session-mode in args. Use requested/configured profiles only; on profile failures run profiles/doctor. Profile content is model-visible.

For agent_browser artifacts, use exact user paths and verify details.artifactVerification/details.artifacts before claiming success. Save details.promptGuard-required artifacts before close; record stop needs ffmpeg; close keeps files; waited:timeout is not proof.

When agent_browser details.nextActions exists, use exact payloads over guessed selectors/prose. Dense snapshots: check Omitted high-value controls/highValueControlRefIds. Dashboards: verify scroll with screenshot/snapshot.

For agent_browser extraction: read <url> for docs/text; read for active-tab DOM; get title/url; get text/html/value/count <selector>; get attr <selector> <name>; eval --stdin for targeted state. Batch 3+ getters; heed visibility warnings.
Prefer agent_browser over bash, osascript, AppleScript, or generic browser-driving shell for sites, docs, clicking, filling, screenshots, eval, and batch workflows.

added.name: agent_browser
added.description: Browse and interact with websites using agent-browser. Use this for web research, reading live docs, opening pages, taking snapshots or screenshots, clicking links, filling forms, extracting page content, and authenticated/profile-based browser work. Input choice: default `args` for open → snapshot -i → click/fill @refs; `semanticAction` for stable role/text/label targets; `job` or `qa` for multi-step checks; `electron` only for desktop apps; experimental `sourceLookup` / `networkSourceLookup` for candidates only.

args.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name and any shell operators. Required unless semanticAction, job, qa, sourceLookup, networkSourceLookup, or electron is provided. Do not include --json (wrapper injects it). Typical first calls: open, snapshot -i, click/fill current @refs, then snapshot -i again after navigation or DOM changes.

items.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name. Do not pass --json; the wrapper injects it. First-call recipe: open → snapshot -i → click/fill @eN → snapshot -i.

action.description: List discovered Electron apps.

maxResults.description: Maximum electron.list apps to return. Defaults to 50; values above 200 are clamped.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter for electron.list across app name, bundle id, desktop id, and paths.

action.description: Launch an Electron app with an isolated wrapper-owned profile.

allow.description: Optional caller-owned allow list for electron.launch policy checks.

items.description: App identifier allowed by the caller for electron.launch.

appArgs.description: Optional Electron app argv. Wrapper-owned lifecycle/debug flags are rejected.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

appPath.description: Electron launch target: macOS .app bundle path. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

targetType.description: Preferred CDP target type. Defaults to page.

launchId.description: Wrapper launch id for electron.probe follow-up targeting.

electron.description: Electron wrapper action. Fields are action-specific and unsupported fields are rejected.

failFast.description: Stop the compiled batch on the first failed job step. Defaults to true so later mutating steps do not run after setup/assertion failures.

action.description: Constrained one-call job step compiled to existing upstream batch commands.

delayMs.description: Optional per-character delay for type steps; when set, the job compiles to focus/keyboard type/wait steps instead of instant fill-like typing, capped at 200 characters.

loadState.description: Optional readiness wait to insert immediately after an open step; use domcontentloaded/load/networkidle when the next job step needs page hydration evidence before clicking or reading.

locator.description: Semantic locator for click/fill steps when selector is omitted.

name.description: Accessible name filter for role locator click/fill steps.

path.description: Artifact/download path for waitForDownload or screenshot steps.

press.description: Optional key to press after a type step, for example Enter.

role.description: Role locator value for click/fill steps when locator is role.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for click/fill/type/select-like steps; omit when using semantic locator fields on click/fill steps.

text.description: Text for fill steps or visible text for assertText steps.

url.description: URL for open steps; exact URL or * / ** glob-style URL pattern for assertUrl steps.

value.description: Single option value for select steps, or locator value for semantic click/fill steps.

values.description: One or more option values for select steps.

items.description: Option value for select steps.

networkSourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: failed-request-to-source candidates only (initiator metadata and bounded workspace URL literals; not definitive blame). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

filter.description: Optional upstream network requests filter pattern.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan for URL literals. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

namespace.description: Optional upstream namespace; prepends --namespace <name> before the generated batch.

requestId.description: Optional network request id to inspect with network request <id>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the generated batch.

url.description: Optional failed request URL or URL fragment to correlate with local source.

outputPath.description: Optional workspace-relative or absolute file path that receives the model-facing command data/result after the browser command completes. Useful for eval/get/snapshot captures that should become durable local artifacts.

attached.description: Run the QA preset against the currently attached session instead of opening qa.url.

checkConsole.description: Whether to inspect console messages and fail on console errors. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkErrors.description: Whether to inspect page errors and fail when errors are present. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

attached.description: When omitted or false, qa.url is required and opened before checks.

checkConsole.description: Whether to fail on console error messages. Defaults to true.

checkErrors.description: Whether to fail on page errors. Defaults to true.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to true.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

url.description: URL to open for a lightweight QA preset.

qa.description: Lightweight QA preset. Use qa.url to open a URL, or qa.attached=true to check the current attached session without opening a URL.

action.description: Intent action to compile to an existing agent-browser find command, direct selector/ref command, or upstream select when action=select.

locator.description: Upstream find locator family to use for check/click/fill actions.

name.description: Accessible name filter for locator=role; compiles to --name <name>.

role.description: Role locator value for locator=role. May be used instead of value; when both are set they must match.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for direct click/check/fill actions, or for select actions compiled to select <selector> <value...>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the compiled command.

value.description: Locator value for find actions, or a single option value for select actions. For locator=role, role may be supplied instead.

values.description: One or more option values for select actions.

items.description: Option value for select actions.

sessionMode.description: Session handling mode. `auto` reuses the extension-managed pi-scoped session when possible. `fresh` switches that managed session to a fresh upstream launch so launch-scoped flags like --namespace, --restore, --restore-save, restore check flags, --profile, --executable-path, --webgpu, --session-name, --cdp, --state, --auto-connect, --init-script, --enable, -p/--provider, or iOS --device apply and later auto calls follow the new browser.

sourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: local UI-to-source candidates only (confidence/evidence, not guaranteed mappings). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

componentName.description: Component name to correlate with react tree output and bounded local workspace search.

includeDomHints.description: Whether selector lookups should inspect DOM HTML attributes for source-like metadata. Defaults to true.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan when componentName is provided. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

reactFiberId.description: React fiber id to inspect with upstream react inspect. Requires a session opened with --enable react-devtools.

selector.description: Visible selector or @ref whose DOM metadata should be inspected for source hints.

stdin.description: Optional raw stdin content; only supported for batch, eval --stdin, auth save --password-stdin, and is generated internally by job, qa, sourceLookup, or networkSourceLookup mode. Do not use with electron mode.

timeoutMs.description: Optional per-call wrapper subprocess watchdog in milliseconds for browser CLI args/job/qa/source lookup calls. Use for long opens or large output captures; explicit long wait steps are forwarded, so set timeoutMs above the wait duration plus a small grace window when overriding the derived watchdog. Electron actions use electron.timeoutMs instead.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

allTools.name: agent_browser
allTools.description: Browse and interact with websites using agent-browser. Use this for web research, reading live docs, opening pages, taking snapshots or screenshots, clicking links, filling forms, extracting page content, and authenticated/profile-based browser work. Input choice: default `args` for open → snapshot -i → click/fill @refs; `semanticAction` for stable role/text/label targets; `job` or `qa` for multi-step checks; `electron` only for desktop apps; experimental `sourceLookup` / `networkSourceLookup` for candidates only.

args.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name and any shell operators. Required unless semanticAction, job, qa, sourceLookup, networkSourceLookup, or electron is provided. Do not include --json (wrapper injects it). Typical first calls: open, snapshot -i, click/fill current @refs, then snapshot -i again after navigation or DOM changes.

items.description: Exact agent-browser CLI arguments, excluding the binary name. Do not pass --json; the wrapper injects it. First-call recipe: open → snapshot -i → click/fill @eN → snapshot -i.

action.description: List discovered Electron apps.

maxResults.description: Maximum electron.list apps to return. Defaults to 50; values above 200 are clamped.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter for electron.list across app name, bundle id, desktop id, and paths.

action.description: Launch an Electron app with an isolated wrapper-owned profile.

allow.description: Optional caller-owned allow list for electron.launch policy checks.

items.description: App identifier allowed by the caller for electron.launch.

appArgs.description: Optional Electron app argv. Wrapper-owned lifecycle/debug flags are rejected.

items.description: Argument passed to the Electron application.

appPath.description: Electron launch target: macOS .app bundle path. Exactly one launch target is required for electron.launch.

deny.description: Optional caller-owned deny list for electron.launch policy checks; deny wins over allow.

items.description: App identifier denied by the caller for electron.launch.

handoff.description: Post-launch handoff depth. Defaults to snapshot.

targetType.description: Preferred CDP target type. Defaults to page.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup one wrapper-tracked Electron launch by launchId.

launchId.description: Wrapper launch id for electron.status and electron.cleanup.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup all wrapper-tracked Electron launches.

all.description: Apply electron.status or electron.cleanup to all wrapper-owned launches.

action.description: Inspect or cleanup the only active wrapper-tracked Electron launch.
Probe the current attached Electron managed session; launchId is accepted for launch-scoped follow-up actions.

launchId.description: Wrapper launch id for electron.probe follow-up targeting.

electron.description: Electron wrapper action. Fields are action-specific and unsupported fields are rejected.

failFast.description: Stop the compiled batch on the first failed job step. Defaults to true so later mutating steps do not run after setup/assertion failures.

action.description: Constrained one-call job step compiled to existing upstream batch commands.

delayMs.description: Optional per-character delay for type steps; when set, the job compiles to focus/keyboard type/wait steps instead of instant fill-like typing, capped at 200 characters.

loadState.description: Optional readiness wait to insert immediately after an open step; use domcontentloaded/load/networkidle when the next job step needs page hydration evidence before clicking or reading.

locator.description: Semantic locator for click/fill steps when selector is omitted.

name.description: Accessible name filter for role locator click/fill steps.

path.description: Artifact/download path for waitForDownload or screenshot steps.

press.description: Optional key to press after a type step, for example Enter.

role.description: Role locator value for click/fill steps when locator is role.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for click/fill/type/select-like steps; omit when using semantic locator fields on click/fill steps.

text.description: Text for fill steps or visible text for assertText steps.

url.description: URL for open steps; exact URL or * / ** glob-style URL pattern for assertUrl steps.

value.description: Single option value for select steps, or locator value for semantic click/fill steps.

values.description: One or more option values for select steps.

items.description: Option value for select steps.

networkSourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: failed-request-to-source candidates only (initiator metadata and bounded workspace URL literals; not definitive blame). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

filter.description: Optional upstream network requests filter pattern.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan for URL literals. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

namespace.description: Optional upstream namespace; prepends --namespace <name> before the generated batch.

requestId.description: Optional network request id to inspect with network request <id>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the generated batch.

url.description: Optional failed request URL or URL fragment to correlate with local source.

outputPath.description: Optional workspace-relative or absolute file path that receives the model-facing command data/result after the browser command completes. Useful for eval/get/snapshot captures that should become durable local artifacts.

attached.description: Run the QA preset against the currently attached session instead of opening qa.url.

checkConsole.description: Whether to inspect console messages and fail on console errors. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkErrors.description: Whether to inspect page errors and fail when errors are present. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to false for qa.attached because upstream buffers may predate the check.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

attached.description: When omitted or false, qa.url is required and opened before checks.

checkConsole.description: Whether to fail on console error messages. Defaults to true.

checkErrors.description: Whether to fail on page errors. Defaults to true.

checkNetwork.description: Whether to inspect network requests and fail on actionable request failures; benign icon misses warn. Defaults to true.

expectedSelector.description: Selector or @ref that must appear on the page.

expectedText.description: Text that must appear on the page.

loadState.description: Page readiness state for the QA preset before assertions and diagnostics. Defaults to domcontentloaded; use networkidle only for pages without long-lived background requests.

screenshotPath.description: Optional evidence screenshot path captured at the end of the QA preset.

url.description: URL to open for a lightweight QA preset.

qa.description: Lightweight QA preset. Use qa.url to open a URL, or qa.attached=true to check the current attached session without opening a URL.

action.description: Intent action to compile to an existing agent-browser find command, direct selector/ref command, or upstream select when action=select.

locator.description: Upstream find locator family to use for check/click/fill actions.

name.description: Accessible name filter for locator=role; compiles to --name <name>.

role.description: Role locator value for locator=role. May be used instead of value; when both are set they must match.

selector.description: Selector or @ref for direct click/check/fill actions, or for select actions compiled to select <selector> <value...>.

session.description: Optional upstream session name; prepends --session <name> before the compiled command.

value.description: Locator value for find actions, or a single option value for select actions. For locator=role, role may be supplied instead.

values.description: One or more option values for select actions.

items.description: Option value for select actions.

sessionMode.description: Session handling mode. `auto` reuses the extension-managed pi-scoped session when possible. `fresh` switches that managed session to a fresh upstream launch so launch-scoped flags like --namespace, --restore, --restore-save, restore check flags, --profile, --executable-path, --webgpu, --session-name, --cdp, --state, --auto-connect, --init-script, --enable, -p/--provider, or iOS --device apply and later auto calls follow the new browser.

sourceLookup.description: EXPERIMENTAL: local UI-to-source candidates only (confidence/evidence, not guaranteed mappings). Compiles to batch; mutually exclusive with other input modes.

componentName.description: Component name to correlate with react tree output and bounded local workspace search.

includeDomHints.description: Whether selector lookups should inspect DOM HTML attributes for source-like metadata. Defaults to true.

maxWorkspaceFiles.description: Maximum local source files to scan when componentName is provided. Defaults to 2000 and cannot exceed 5000.

reactFiberId.description: React fiber id to inspect with upstream react inspect. Requires a session opened with --enable react-devtools.

selector.description: Visible selector or @ref whose DOM metadata should be inspected for source hints.

stdin.description: Optional raw stdin content; only supported for batch, eval --stdin, auth save --password-stdin, and is generated internally by job, qa, sourceLookup, or networkSourceLookup mode. Do not use with electron mode.

timeoutMs.description: Optional per-call wrapper subprocess watchdog in milliseconds for browser CLI args/job/qa/source lookup calls. Use for long opens or large output captures; explicit long wait steps are forwarded, so set timeoutMs above the wait duration plus a small grace window when overriding the derived watchdog. Electron actions use electron.timeoutMs instead.

promptGuidelines: Use agent_browser for real browser or live web content.

For detailed agent_browser docs, read targeted sections: /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/README.md (setup), /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/docs/COMMAND_REFERENCE.md (commands), /packages/node_modules/pi-agent-browser-native/docs/TOOL_CONTRACT.md (result/details). Do not load the full command reference unless needed.

Use agent_browser with one input mode: args, semanticAction, job, qa, sourceLookup/networkSourceLookup, or electron. stdin only for batch/eval/auth/wrapper batch; electron rejects stdin; never pass --json.

For agent_browser, use open → snapshot -i → current @refs or semanticAction → re-snapshot after navigation/scroll/rerender. Batch same-snapshot forms; split before navigation/submits. Stop before order/post/purchase/submit.

Use agent_browser sessionMode=fresh for launch-scoped flags, including --allowed-domains; never put --session-mode in args. Use requested/configured profiles only; on profile failures run profiles/doctor. Profile content is model-visible.

For agent_browser artifacts, use exact user paths and verify details.artifactVerification/details.artifacts before claiming success. Save details.promptGuard-required artifacts before close; record stop needs ffmpeg; close keeps files; waited:timeout is not proof.

When agent_browser details.nextActions exists, use exact payloads over guessed selectors/prose. Dense snapshots: check Omitted high-value controls/highValueControlRefIds. Dashboards: verify scroll with screenshot/snapshot.

For agent_browser extraction: read <url> for docs/text; read for active-tab DOM; get title/url; get text/html/value/count <selector>; get attr <selector> <name>; eval --stdin for targeted state. Batch 3+ getters; heed visibility warnings.
Prefer agent_browser over bash, osascript, AppleScript, or generic browser-driving shell for sites, docs, clicking, filling, screenshots, eval, and batch workflows.

allTools.name: bash

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

path.description: Absolute or relative file path

added.name: readSeek_def
added.description: Find symbol declarations by qualified or unqualified name. Use instead of text search when locating where a function, class, type, or other symbol is defined.

cached.description: In a Git repository, search tracked/indexed files

ignored.description: With others=true, include ignored untracked files

lang.description: Language override when auto-detection is ambiguous

name.description: Qualified or unqualified symbol name

others.description: In a Git repository, search untracked files

added.name: readSeek_edit
added.description: Edit existing text files safely with fresh `LINE:HASH` anchors; on `file-not-read`, read or search the file first.

edits.description: Edits: set_line, replace_lines, insert_after, replace_symbol, or replace

path.description: Absolute or relative file path

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

added.name: Agent
added.description: Launch a real pi-crew subagent. Uses pi-crew's durable child-process runtime by default; set run_in_background=true for parallel/background work, then use get_subagent_result.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Background agents sharing the same batch_id receive ONE consolidated completion notification when ALL members finish (instead of N individual notifications). Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

max_turns.description: Reserved for live-session subagents; child-process runtime may ignore this.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

subagent_type.description: pi-crew agent name, e.g. explorer, planner, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, test-engineer.

promptGuidelines: Use Agent for independent exploration, review, verification, or implementation subtasks instead of doing all work in the parent turn.
For parallel work, launch multiple Agent calls with run_in_background=true, then call get_subagent_result for each result.
Available pi-crew subagent types include explorer, planner, analyst, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, and test-engineer.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

added.name: crew_agent_result
added.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

added.name: crew_agent_steer
added.description: Send a steering note to a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

added.name: get_subagent_result
added.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew background subagent.

added.name: steer_subagent
added.description: Send a steering note to a running pi-crew subagent. Live-session steering is planned; child-process runs expose durable status and can be cancelled if needed.

added.name: team
added.description:
Coordinate Pi teams. Use proactively for complex multi-file work, planning, implementation, tests, reviews, security audits, research, async/background runs, and worktree-isolated execution. Use action='recommend' when unsure which team/workflow to choose. Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation.

ℹ️  ADVISORY NOTE (preflight, never blocks): pi-crew prints informational notes about workflow topology. Review the note, then decide. There is no BLOCK — agents always exercise judgment.
- Single-task run: raw `Agent` tool would be ~30× faster and ~5× cheaper. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- 2–3 step sequential run: measured 5.7× slower than raw `Agent` calls in Run #3. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- ≥3 concurrent or complex DAG: validated good use case, pi-crew notes 'validated use case'.
If unsure, call { action: 'recommend', goal } first to get a team/workflow suggestion.

action.description: Team action. Defaults to 'list' when omitted.

agent.description: Agent name to inspect or run directly.

analysis.description: Inline analysis/context notes from the calling session. Persisted to artifacts/{runId}/shared/analysis.md (audit trail) and auto-injected into any workflow step declaring reads: analysis.md (e.g. builtin 'plan-execute'). Mutually exclusive with analysisPath. Ignored by goal-wrapped runs and chain dispatch in v1.

analysisPath.description: Path to an existing markdown analysis file (resolved within cwd). Copied into shared/analysis.md. Mutually exclusive with analysis.

async.description: Run in background when execution support is enabled.

budgetAbort.description: Budget abort threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.95 (95%). Aborts further execution when this threshold is crossed.

budgetTotal.description: Total token budget for the run. When set, enables budget tracking with default 80% warning and 95% abort thresholds. Minimum 1000 — this is a MISCONFIGURATION GUARD (catches typos / silent-abort configs like budgetTotal:1, which would abort on turn 1), NOT a usefulness guarantee; a productive multi-turn goal needs far more than 1000 tokens.

budgetUnlimited.description: When true, skip budget enforcement entirely (explicit opt-out). Goal-start validation requires budgetTotal>=1000 OR budgetUnlimited:true; audit-logged when set. The validation itself is enforced in a later integration task.

budgetWarning.description: Budget warning threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.8 (80%). Emits warning event when this threshold is crossed.

chain.description: Chain expression: "step1 -> step2 -> step3". Runs each step as a sequential team run, passing handoff context forward via the goal text. Supports inline goals ("...") and @team references. e.g. chain='"Research X" -> "Analyze" -> "Write report"'.

context.description: Child context mode for workers.

cron.description: Cron expression for recurring scheduled runs (e.g., '0 9 * * MON').

details.description: (status) Output detail level. true (default) = full status (task graph, agents, effectiveness, events). false = compact summary (status, goal, task counts, and only failed/attention task errors) for quick checks.

dryRun.description: Preview a management mutation without writing files.

excludeContextBash.description: Mark certain bash commands as excludeFromContext to reduce context tokens (default: false).

focus.description: Sub-focus for the doctor action. 'zombies' runs a READ-ONLY scan for orphaned pi-crew sub-agent processes (identified by PI_CREW_KIND=subagent); it never kills and never matches the user's interactive main session.

force.description: Override reference checks for destructive management actions.

goal.description: High-level objective for a team run.

interval.description: Interval in milliseconds between recurring scheduled runs.

jobId.description: Job ID for schedule management actions.

keep.description: Number of finished runs to keep for prune.

message.description: Message for respond action.

model.description: Model override for direct runs.

once.description: ISO timestamp or epoch ms for a one-time scheduled run.

planPath.description: Path to a markdown plan document for orchestration.

replyDeadline.description: Ms epoch deadline for a reply.

replyTo.description: ID of the original mailbox message this is a reply to.

resource.description: Resource kind for get/create/update/delete/list. Defaults to all for list.

role.description: Role name to run directly within a team.

runId.description: Run ID for status, cancel, or resume.

runKind.description: Background dispatch discriminator. Default "team-run" runs the normal executeTeamRun workflow; "goal-loop" (P0/P1) and "dynamic-workflow" (P2/P3) dispatch to their respective background runners. Absent = "team-run" for backward compatibility.

scope.description: Resource scope for discovery or management.

singleAgent.description: When true (with action=plan), compose a single-agent sequential prompt for the workflow instead of a multi-agent plan. Cliff-resilient mode.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to add to role/default skills, an array of skill names, or false to disable all injected skills for this run.

subAction.description: Sub-action for schedule management (remove, disable, enable, update).

task.description: Concrete task text for direct role/agent execution.

team.description: Team name, e.g. default or implementation.

tokenBudget.description: Per-workflow token budget for dynamic-workflow runs. When set, ctx.agent() auto-rejects with ok:false once exhausted. Accumulated from each agent run's reported usage. Overrides workflow.maxTokenBudget.

updateReferences.description: When renaming agents or workflows, update team references in the same project/user scope.

workflow.description: Workflow name, e.g. default or review.

workspaceMode.description: Workspace isolation mode. Worktree mode is planned after MVP.

budgetUnlimited.description: When true, skip budget enforcement entirely (explicit opt-out). Goal-start validation requires budgetTotal>=1000 OR budgetUnlimited:true; audit-logged when set. The validation itself is enforced in a later integration task.

budgetWarning.description: Budget warning threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.8 (80%). Emits warning event when this threshold is crossed.

chain.description: Chain expression: "step1 -> step2 -> step3". Runs each step as a sequential team run, passing handoff context forward via the goal text. Supports inline goals ("...") and @team references. e.g. chain='"Research X" -> "Analyze" -> "Write report"'.

context.description: Child context mode for workers.

cron.description: Cron expression for recurring scheduled runs (e.g., '0 9 * * MON').

details.description: (status) Output detail level. true (default) = full status (task graph, agents, effectiveness, events). false = compact summary (status, goal, task counts, and only failed/attention task errors) for quick checks.

dryRun.description: Preview a management mutation without writing files.

excludeContextBash.description: Mark certain bash commands as excludeFromContext to reduce context tokens (default: false).

focus.description: Sub-focus for the doctor action. 'zombies' runs a READ-ONLY scan for orphaned pi-crew sub-agent processes (identified by PI_CREW_KIND=subagent); it never kills and never matches the user's interactive main session.

force.description: Override reference checks for destructive management actions.

goal.description: High-level objective for a team run.

interval.description: Interval in milliseconds between recurring scheduled runs.

jobId.description: Job ID for schedule management actions.

keep.description: Number of finished runs to keep for prune.

message.description: Message for respond action.

model.description: Model override for direct runs.

once.description: ISO timestamp or epoch ms for a one-time scheduled run.

planPath.description: Path to a markdown plan document for orchestration.

replyDeadline.description: Ms epoch deadline for a reply.

replyTo.description: ID of the original mailbox message this is a reply to.

resource.description: Resource kind for get/create/update/delete/list. Defaults to all for list.

role.description: Role name to run directly within a team.

runId.description: Run ID for status, cancel, or resume.

runKind.description: Background dispatch discriminator. Default "team-run" runs the normal executeTeamRun workflow; "goal-loop" (P0/P1) and "dynamic-workflow" (P2/P3) dispatch to their respective background runners. Absent = "team-run" for backward compatibility.

scope.description: Resource scope for discovery or management.

singleAgent.description: When true (with action=plan), compose a single-agent sequential prompt for the workflow instead of a multi-agent plan. Cliff-resilient mode.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to add to role/default skills, an array of skill names, or false to disable all injected skills for this run.

subAction.description: Sub-action for schedule management (remove, disable, enable, update).

task.description: Concrete task text for direct role/agent execution.

team.description: Team name, e.g. default or implementation.

tokenBudget.description: Per-workflow token budget for dynamic-workflow runs. When set, ctx.agent() auto-rejects with ok:false once exhausted. Accumulated from each agent run's reported usage. Overrides workflow.maxTokenBudget.

updateReferences.description: When renaming agents or workflows, update team references in the same project/user scope.

workflow.description: Workflow name, e.g. default or review.

workspaceMode.description: Workspace isolation mode. Worktree mode is planned after MVP.
