allTools.name: Agent
allTools.description: Launch a real pi-crew subagent. Uses pi-crew's durable child-process runtime by default; set run_in_background=true for parallel/background work, then use get_subagent_result.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Background agents sharing the same batch_id receive ONE consolidated completion notification when ALL members finish (instead of N individual notifications). Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

max_turns.description: Reserved for live-session subagents; child-process runtime may ignore this.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

subagent_type.description: pi-crew agent name, e.g. explorer, planner, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, test-engineer.

promptGuidelines: Use Agent for independent exploration, review, verification, or implementation subtasks instead of doing all work in the parent turn.
For parallel work, launch multiple Agent calls with run_in_background=true, then call get_subagent_result for each result.
Available pi-crew subagent types include explorer, planner, analyst, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, and test-engineer.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: crew_agent
allTools.description: Launch a real pi-crew subagent using a conflict-safe pi-crew-specific tool name.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Background agents sharing the same batch_id receive ONE consolidated completion notification when ALL members finish (instead of N individual notifications). Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

max_turns.description: Reserved for live-session subagents; child-process runtime may ignore this.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

subagent_type.description: pi-crew agent name, e.g. explorer, planner, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, test-engineer.

promptGuidelines: Use Agent for independent exploration, review, verification, or implementation subtasks instead of doing all work in the parent turn.
For parallel work, launch multiple Agent calls with run_in_background=true, then call get_subagent_result for each result.
Available pi-crew subagent types include explorer, planner, analyst, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, and test-engineer.

allTools.name: crew_agent_result
allTools.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

allTools.name: crew_agent_steer
allTools.description: Send a steering note to a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: get_subagent_result
allTools.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew background subagent.

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: steer_subagent
allTools.description: Send a steering note to a running pi-crew subagent. Live-session steering is planned; child-process runs expose durable status and can be cancelled if needed.

allTools.name: team
allTools.description:
Coordinate Pi teams. Use proactively for complex multi-file work, planning, implementation, tests, reviews, security audits, research, async/background runs, and worktree-isolated execution. Use action='recommend' when unsure which team/workflow to choose. Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation.

ℹ️  ADVISORY NOTE (preflight, never blocks): pi-crew prints informational notes about workflow topology. Review the note, then decide. There is no BLOCK — agents always exercise judgment.
- Single-task run: raw `Agent` tool would be ~30× faster and ~5× cheaper. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- 2–3 step sequential run: measured 5.7× slower than raw `Agent` calls in Run #3. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- ≥3 concurrent or complex DAG: validated good use case, pi-crew notes 'validated use case'.
If unsure, call { action: 'recommend', goal } first to get a team/workflow suggestion.

action.description: Team action. Defaults to 'list' when omitted.

agent.description: Agent name to inspect or run directly.

analysis.description: Inline analysis/context notes from the calling session. Persisted to artifacts/{runId}/shared/analysis.md (audit trail) and auto-injected into any workflow step declaring reads: analysis.md (e.g. builtin 'plan-execute'). Mutually exclusive with analysisPath. Ignored by goal-wrapped runs and chain dispatch in v1.

analysisPath.description: Path to an existing markdown analysis file (resolved within cwd). Copied into shared/analysis.md. Mutually exclusive with analysis.

async.description: Run in background when execution support is enabled.

budgetAbort.description: Budget abort threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.95 (95%). Aborts further execution when this threshold is crossed.

budgetTotal.description: Total token budget for the run. When set, enables budget tracking with default 80% warning and 95% abort thresholds. Minimum 1000 — this is a MISCONFIGURATION GUARD (catches typos / silent-abort configs like budgetTotal:1, which would abort on turn 1), NOT a usefulness guarantee; a productive multi-turn goal needs far more than 1000 tokens.

budgetUnlimited.description: When true, skip budget enforcement entirely (explicit opt-out). Goal-start validation requires budgetTotal>=1000 OR budgetUnlimited:true; audit-logged when set. The validation itself is enforced in a later integration task.

budgetWarning.description: Budget warning threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.8 (80%). Emits warning event when this threshold is crossed.

chain.description: Chain expression: "step1 -> step2 -> step3". Runs each step as a sequential team run, passing handoff context forward via the goal text. Supports inline goals ("...") and @team references. e.g. chain='"Research X" -> "Analyze" -> "Write report"'.

context.description: Child context mode for workers.

cron.description: Cron expression for recurring scheduled runs (e.g., '0 9 * * MON').

details.description: (status) Output detail level. true (default) = full status (task graph, agents, effectiveness, events). false = compact summary (status, goal, task counts, and only failed/attention task errors) for quick checks.

dryRun.description: Preview a management mutation without writing files.

excludeContextBash.description: Mark certain bash commands as excludeFromContext to reduce context tokens (default: false).

focus.description: Sub-focus for the doctor action. 'zombies' runs a READ-ONLY scan for orphaned pi-crew sub-agent processes (identified by PI_CREW_KIND=subagent); it never kills and never matches the user's interactive main session.

force.description: Override reference checks for destructive management actions.

goal.description: High-level objective for a team run.

interval.description: Interval in milliseconds between recurring scheduled runs.

jobId.description: Job ID for schedule management actions.

keep.description: Number of finished runs to keep for prune.

message.description: Message for respond action.

model.description: Model override for direct runs.

once.description: ISO timestamp or epoch ms for a one-time scheduled run.

planPath.description: Path to a markdown plan document for orchestration.

replyDeadline.description: Ms epoch deadline for a reply.

replyTo.description: ID of the original mailbox message this is a reply to.

resource.description: Resource kind for get/create/update/delete/list. Defaults to all for list.

role.description: Role name to run directly within a team.

runId.description: Run ID for status, cancel, or resume.

runKind.description: Background dispatch discriminator. Default "team-run" runs the normal executeTeamRun workflow; "goal-loop" (P0/P1) and "dynamic-workflow" (P2/P3) dispatch to their respective background runners. Absent = "team-run" for backward compatibility.

scope.description: Resource scope for discovery or management.

singleAgent.description: When true (with action=plan), compose a single-agent sequential prompt for the workflow instead of a multi-agent plan. Cliff-resilient mode.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to add to role/default skills, an array of skill names, or false to disable all injected skills for this run.

subAction.description: Sub-action for schedule management (remove, disable, enable, update).

task.description: Concrete task text for direct role/agent execution.

team.description: Team name, e.g. default or implementation.

tokenBudget.description: Per-workflow token budget for dynamic-workflow runs. When set, ctx.agent() auto-rejects with ok:false once exhausted. Accumulated from each agent run's reported usage. Overrides workflow.maxTokenBudget.

updateReferences.description: When renaming agents or workflows, update team references in the same project/user scope.

workflow.description: Workflow name, e.g. default or review.

workspaceMode.description: Workspace isolation mode. Worktree mode is planned after MVP.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

allTools.name: Agent
allTools.description: Launch a real pi-crew subagent. Uses pi-crew's durable child-process runtime by default; set run_in_background=true for parallel/background work, then use get_subagent_result.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Background agents sharing the same batch_id receive ONE consolidated completion notification when ALL members finish (instead of N individual notifications). Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

max_turns.description: Reserved for live-session subagents; child-process runtime may ignore this.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

subagent_type.description: pi-crew agent name, e.g. explorer, planner, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, test-engineer.

promptGuidelines: Use Agent for independent exploration, review, verification, or implementation subtasks instead of doing all work in the parent turn.
For parallel work, launch multiple Agent calls with run_in_background=true, then call get_subagent_result for each result.
Available pi-crew subagent types include explorer, planner, analyst, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, and test-engineer.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: crew_agent
allTools.description: Launch a real pi-crew subagent using a conflict-safe pi-crew-specific tool name.

batch_id.description: Optional batch grouping id. Background agents sharing the same batch_id receive ONE consolidated completion notification when ALL members finish (instead of N individual notifications). Use this when launching several background agents in one turn and you do not join them immediately. Omit for the default individual-notification behavior.

description.description: Short 3-5 word task description.

max_turns.description: Reserved for live-session subagents; child-process runtime may ignore this.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, pi-crew uses Pi-configured model fallback.

prompt.description: The task for the subagent to perform.

run_in_background.description: Run in background and return an agent ID immediately.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to inject for this subagent, or false to disable selected/default skills.

subagent_type.description: pi-crew agent name, e.g. explorer, planner, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, test-engineer.

promptGuidelines: Use Agent for independent exploration, review, verification, or implementation subtasks instead of doing all work in the parent turn.
For parallel work, launch multiple Agent calls with run_in_background=true, then call get_subagent_result for each result.
Available pi-crew subagent types include explorer, planner, analyst, executor, reviewer, verifier, writer, security-reviewer, and test-engineer.

allTools.name: crew_agent_result
allTools.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

allTools.name: crew_agent_steer
allTools.description: Send a steering note to a pi-crew subagent using the conflict-safe tool name.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: get_subagent_result
allTools.description: Check status and retrieve results from a pi-crew background subagent.

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: steer_subagent
allTools.description: Send a steering note to a running pi-crew subagent. Live-session steering is planned; child-process runs expose durable status and can be cancelled if needed.

allTools.name: team
allTools.description:
Coordinate Pi teams. Use proactively for complex multi-file work, planning, implementation, tests, reviews, security audits, research, async/background runs, and worktree-isolated execution. Use action='recommend' when unsure which team/workflow to choose. Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation.

ℹ️  ADVISORY NOTE (preflight, never blocks): pi-crew prints informational notes about workflow topology. Review the note, then decide. There is no BLOCK — agents always exercise judgment.
- Single-task run: raw `Agent` tool would be ~30× faster and ~5× cheaper. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- 2–3 step sequential run: measured 5.7× slower than raw `Agent` calls in Run #3. pi-crew notes this and proceeds anyway.
- ≥3 concurrent or complex DAG: validated good use case, pi-crew notes 'validated use case'.
If unsure, call { action: 'recommend', goal } first to get a team/workflow suggestion.

action.description: Team action. Defaults to 'list' when omitted.

agent.description: Agent name to inspect or run directly.

analysis.description: Inline analysis/context notes from the calling session. Persisted to artifacts/{runId}/shared/analysis.md (audit trail) and auto-injected into any workflow step declaring reads: analysis.md (e.g. builtin 'plan-execute'). Mutually exclusive with analysisPath. Ignored by goal-wrapped runs and chain dispatch in v1.

analysisPath.description: Path to an existing markdown analysis file (resolved within cwd). Copied into shared/analysis.md. Mutually exclusive with analysis.

async.description: Run in background when execution support is enabled.

budgetAbort.description: Budget abort threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.95 (95%). Aborts further execution when this threshold is crossed.

budgetTotal.description: Total token budget for the run. When set, enables budget tracking with default 80% warning and 95% abort thresholds. Minimum 1000 — this is a MISCONFIGURATION GUARD (catches typos / silent-abort configs like budgetTotal:1, which would abort on turn 1), NOT a usefulness guarantee; a productive multi-turn goal needs far more than 1000 tokens.

budgetUnlimited.description: When true, skip budget enforcement entirely (explicit opt-out). Goal-start validation requires budgetTotal>=1000 OR budgetUnlimited:true; audit-logged when set. The validation itself is enforced in a later integration task.

budgetWarning.description: Budget warning threshold as a fraction (0-1). Default: 0.8 (80%). Emits warning event when this threshold is crossed.

chain.description: Chain expression: "step1 -> step2 -> step3". Runs each step as a sequential team run, passing handoff context forward via the goal text. Supports inline goals ("...") and @team references. e.g. chain='"Research X" -> "Analyze" -> "Write report"'.

context.description: Child context mode for workers.

cron.description: Cron expression for recurring scheduled runs (e.g., '0 9 * * MON').

details.description: (status) Output detail level. true (default) = full status (task graph, agents, effectiveness, events). false = compact summary (status, goal, task counts, and only failed/attention task errors) for quick checks.

dryRun.description: Preview a management mutation without writing files.

excludeContextBash.description: Mark certain bash commands as excludeFromContext to reduce context tokens (default: false).

focus.description: Sub-focus for the doctor action. 'zombies' runs a READ-ONLY scan for orphaned pi-crew sub-agent processes (identified by PI_CREW_KIND=subagent); it never kills and never matches the user's interactive main session.

force.description: Override reference checks for destructive management actions.

goal.description: High-level objective for a team run.

interval.description: Interval in milliseconds between recurring scheduled runs.

jobId.description: Job ID for schedule management actions.

keep.description: Number of finished runs to keep for prune.

message.description: Message for respond action.

model.description: Model override for direct runs.

once.description: ISO timestamp or epoch ms for a one-time scheduled run.

planPath.description: Path to a markdown plan document for orchestration.

replyDeadline.description: Ms epoch deadline for a reply.

replyTo.description: ID of the original mailbox message this is a reply to.

resource.description: Resource kind for get/create/update/delete/list. Defaults to all for list.

role.description: Role name to run directly within a team.

runId.description: Run ID for status, cancel, or resume.

runKind.description: Background dispatch discriminator. Default "team-run" runs the normal executeTeamRun workflow; "goal-loop" (P0/P1) and "dynamic-workflow" (P2/P3) dispatch to their respective background runners. Absent = "team-run" for backward compatibility.

scope.description: Resource scope for discovery or management.

singleAgent.description: When true (with action=plan), compose a single-agent sequential prompt for the workflow instead of a multi-agent plan. Cliff-resilient mode.

skill.description: Skill name(s) to add to role/default skills, an array of skill names, or false to disable all injected skills for this run.

subAction.description: Sub-action for schedule management (remove, disable, enable, update).

task.description: Concrete task text for direct role/agent execution.

team.description: Team name, e.g. default or implementation.

tokenBudget.description: Per-workflow token budget for dynamic-workflow runs. When set, ctx.agent() auto-rejects with ok:false once exhausted. Accumulated from each agent run's reported usage. Overrides workflow.maxTokenBudget.

updateReferences.description: When renaming agents or workflows, update team references in the same project/user scope.

workflow.description: Workflow name, e.g. default or review.

workspaceMode.description: Workspace isolation mode. Worktree mode is planned after MVP.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: task
allTools.description:
Delegate a task to a sandboxed Pi RPC process.

Available subagents:
explore: Fast read-focused agent for exploring codebases
general: General-purpose agent for researching and executing complex tasks

background.description: Run without blocking the parent task

command.description: The command that originated this task

description.description: A short 3-5 word description of the task

prompt.description: The complete task for the subagent

task_id.description: An existing task ID to continue

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

added.name: fabric_exec
added.description: Execute type-checked TypeScript through Fabric's configured executor for Pi core tools, MCP, Fabric providers, discovery, and extensions. QuickJS is isolated by default; the optional Node process is an unsafe trusted-code escape hatch. In full code mode, and always in Schema enforce mode, this is the exclusive model tool path.

agentBudget.description: Optional agent-call cap, bounded by Fabric configuration

code.description: TypeScript function body. Top-level await and return are supported. Globals include `tools`, `mcp`, `memory`, `state`, `schema`, `compact`, `agents`, `mesh`, `print`, and `π`; full-code mode adds `pi` and `extensions`. See session guidance / `fabric-exec` skill for exact signatures.

description.description: Compact objective shown in the Fabric dashboard

name.description: Human-readable name for the Fabric activity panel

strings.description: Named strings exposed as π.key, useful for content that is awkward to quote

tokenBudget.description: Optional token budget observed by workflow.agent() calls

promptGuidelines: Batch independent operations in one `fabric_exec` program (`Promise.all` for parallel, sequential `await` for ordered), not one call per tool; keep dependent/conditional steps sequential. Return only the compact final value; intermediate results stay in the sandbox.

added.name: fabric
added.description: Open the Fabric dashboard; inspect, reload, or manage agents and actors

added.name: skill:fabric-advisor
added.description: Starts a persistent Pi Fabric peer advisor that reviews the main agent at decision points and surfaces only concrete, material advice. Use for ambient correctness review without another extension.

added.name: skill:fabric-ambient
added.description: Creates a persistent Pi Fabric supervisor or advisor profile. Use for ambient supervision, ongoing peer review, an advisor, or a goal watcher without another extension.

added.name: skill:fabric-council
added.description: Runs a bounded multi-perspective Pi Fabric council with independent reviewers and best-effort synthesis. Use for architecture choices, plans, reviews, and adversarial cross-checking.

added.name: skill:fabric-exec
added.description: Reference for `fabric_exec` TypeScript programs: Pi core tool signatures, discovery, provider and MCP proxies, named strings, return shapes, and schema-driven error recovery. Load before the first Fabric call or after an argument-shape error.

added.name: skill:fabric-fusion
added.description: Multi-model deliberation. Two to 8 distinct models answer in parallel with web-capable tools, then a judge compares consensus, contradictions, coverage gaps, unique insights, and blind spots. Use when the cost of being wrong justifies multiple completions.

added.name: skill:fabric-guide
added.description: Recommends the right user-invoked Pi Fabric workflow without running it. Use when you want help choosing among workflow, council, fusion, RLM, Schema, ambient actors, or swarm.

added.name: skill:fabric-rlm
added.description: Recursively decomposes oversized tasks into bounded child Pi agents with fresh context windows. Use for whole-repo audits, massive-context analysis, and multi-file refactors that do not fit one context.

added.name: skill:fabric-schema
added.description: Uses Fabric's typed Schema evidence loop and, when enabled, its bounded local-file transaction channel. Use when surprise must void a plan and mutation claims need explicit postconditions.

added.name: skill:fabric-supervisor
added.description: Starts a persistent Pi Fabric supervisor that watches the main session toward a concrete goal and steers only when needed. Use for long-running goal supervision without another extension.

added.name: skill:fabric-swarm
added.description: Creates a self-organizing team of persistent Pi Fabric actors with durable topics, mailboxes, and compare-and-swap tasks. Use for messenger-like collaboration and long-lived delegated work.

added.name: skill:fabric-workflow
added.description: Runs a dynamic Pi Fabric workflow with code-held phases, fan-out, pipelines, structured agents, and best-effort verification. Use for large audits, migrations, parallel research, or explicit workflow requests.

rpcCommands.description: Runs a bounded multi-perspective Pi Fabric council with independent reviewers and best-effort synthesis. Use for architecture choices, plans, reviews, and adversarial cross-checking.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-exec
rpcCommands.description: Reference for `fabric_exec` TypeScript programs: Pi core tool signatures, discovery, provider and MCP proxies, named strings, return shapes, and schema-driven error recovery. Load before the first Fabric call or after an argument-shape error.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-fusion
rpcCommands.description: Multi-model deliberation. Two to 8 distinct models answer in parallel with web-capable tools, then a judge compares consensus, contradictions, coverage gaps, unique insights, and blind spots. Use when the cost of being wrong justifies multiple completions.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-guide
rpcCommands.description: Recommends the right user-invoked Pi Fabric workflow without running it. Use when you want help choosing among workflow, council, fusion, RLM, Schema, ambient actors, or swarm.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-rlm
rpcCommands.description: Recursively decomposes oversized tasks into bounded child Pi agents with fresh context windows. Use for whole-repo audits, massive-context analysis, and multi-file refactors that do not fit one context.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-schema
rpcCommands.description: Uses Fabric's typed Schema evidence loop and, when enabled, its bounded local-file transaction channel. Use when surprise must void a plan and mutation claims need explicit postconditions.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-supervisor
rpcCommands.description: Starts a persistent Pi Fabric supervisor that watches the main session toward a concrete goal and steers only when needed. Use for long-running goal supervision without another extension.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-swarm
rpcCommands.description: Creates a self-organizing team of persistent Pi Fabric actors with durable topics, mailboxes, and compare-and-swap tasks. Use for messenger-like collaboration and long-lived delegated work.

rpcCommands.name: skill:fabric-workflow
rpcCommands.description: Runs a dynamic Pi Fabric workflow with code-held phases, fan-out, pipelines, structured agents, and best-effort verification. Use for large audits, migrations, parallel research, or explicit workflow requests.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: fabric_exec
allTools.description: Execute type-checked TypeScript through Fabric's configured executor for Pi core tools, MCP, Fabric providers, discovery, and extensions. QuickJS is isolated by default; the optional Node process is an unsafe trusted-code escape hatch. In full code mode, and always in Schema enforce mode, this is the exclusive model tool path.

agentBudget.description: Optional agent-call cap, bounded by Fabric configuration

code.description: TypeScript function body. Top-level await and return are supported. Globals include `tools`, `mcp`, `memory`, `state`, `schema`, `compact`, `agents`, `mesh`, `print`, and `π`; full-code mode adds `pi` and `extensions`. See session guidance / `fabric-exec` skill for exact signatures.

description.description: Compact objective shown in the Fabric dashboard

name.description: Human-readable name for the Fabric activity panel

strings.description: Named strings exposed as π.key, useful for content that is awkward to quote

tokenBudget.description: Optional token budget observed by workflow.agent() calls

promptGuidelines: Batch independent operations in one `fabric_exec` program (`Promise.all` for parallel, sequential `await` for ordered), not one call per tool; keep dependent/conditional steps sequential. Return only the compact final value; intermediate results stay in the sandbox.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

added.name: intercom
added.description:
Send a message to another pi session running on this machine.
Use this to communicate findings, request help, or coordinate work with other sessions.

Usage:
  intercom({ action: "list" })                    → List active sessions
  intercom({ action: "send", to: "session-name", message: "..." })  → Send message
  intercom({ action: "ask", to: "session-name", message: "..." })   → Ask and wait for reply
  intercom({ action: "reply", message: "..." })                      → Reply to the active/single pending ask
  intercom({ action: "pending" })                                      → List unresolved inbound asks
  intercom({ action: "status" })                  → Show connection status

action.description: Action: 'list', 'send', 'ask', 'reply', 'pending', or 'status'

message.description: Message to send (for 'send', 'ask', or 'reply' action)

replyTo.description: Message ID to reply to (for threading or responding to an 'ask')

to.description: Target session name or ID (for 'send', 'ask', or disambiguating 'reply')

added.name: intercom

added.name: intercom
added.description:
Send a message to another pi session running on this machine.
Use this to communicate findings, request help, or coordinate work with other sessions.

Usage:
  intercom({ action: "list" })                    → List active sessions
  intercom({ action: "send", to: "session-name", message: "..." })  → Send message
  intercom({ action: "ask", to: "session-name", message: "..." })   → Ask and wait for reply
  intercom({ action: "reply", message: "..." })                      → Reply to the active/single pending ask
  intercom({ action: "pending" })                                      → List unresolved inbound asks
  intercom({ action: "status" })                  → Show connection status

action.description: Action: 'list', 'send', 'ask', 'reply', 'pending', or 'status'

message.description: Message to send (for 'send', 'ask', or 'reply' action)

replyTo.description: Message ID to reply to (for threading or responding to an 'ask')

to.description: Target session name or ID (for 'send', 'ask', or disambiguating 'reply')

added.name: intercom
added.description: Open session intercom overlay

added.name: intercom
added.description: Open session intercom overlay

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: intercom
allTools.description:
Send a message to another pi session running on this machine.
Use this to communicate findings, request help, or coordinate work with other sessions.

Usage:
  intercom({ action: "list" })                    → List active sessions
  intercom({ action: "send", to: "session-name", message: "..." })  → Send message
  intercom({ action: "ask", to: "session-name", message: "..." })   → Ask and wait for reply
  intercom({ action: "reply", message: "..." })                      → Reply to the active/single pending ask
  intercom({ action: "pending" })                                      → List unresolved inbound asks
  intercom({ action: "status" })                  → Show connection status

action.description: Action: 'list', 'send', 'ask', 'reply', 'pending', or 'status'

message.description: Message to send (for 'send', 'ask', or 'reply' action)

replyTo.description: Message ID to reply to (for threading or responding to an 'ask')

to.description: Target session name or ID (for 'send', 'ask', or disambiguating 'reply')

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

added.name: confluence_add_comment
added.description: Add a footer comment to a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_add_label
added.description: Add labels to a Confluence page. Uses verified Confluence v1 label endpoint because v2 has no equivalent.

added.name: confluence_create_page
added.description: Create a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_delete_page
added.description: Delete a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_get_comments
added.description: Get footer comments for a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_get_labels
added.description: Get labels for a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_get_page
added.description: Get a Confluence page by ID.

added.name: confluence_get_page_children
added.description: Get child pages for a Confluence page.

added.name: confluence_list_pages
added.description: List Confluence pages.

added.name: confluence_list_spaces
added.description: List Confluence spaces.

added.name: confluence_page
added.description: Fetch compact Confluence page context by URL or page ID.
promptGuidelines: Use confluence_page when Jira or the user links to a Confluence page that may contain requirements or design references.

added.name: confluence_search
added.description: Search Confluence content using CQL. Uses verified Confluence v1 search because v2 has no equivalent.

added.name: confluence_search_user
added.description: Search Confluence users. Uses verified Confluence v1 user search because v2 has no equivalent.

added.name: confluence_update_page
added.description: Update a Confluence page. Pass the current page version; the tool sends current version + 1 to Confluence.

added.name: jira_add_comment
added.description: Add a Jira issue comment. Plain text is converted to Atlassian Document Format.

added.name: jira_add_worklog
added.description: Add a Jira worklog. started must look like 2026-05-04T09:30:00.000+0000.

added.name: jira_batch_create_issues
added.description: Create multiple Jira issues. Plain-text descriptions are converted to Atlassian Document Format.

added.name: jira_batch_create_versions
added.description: Create multiple project versions by calling the verified create-version endpoint for each item.

added.name: jira_batch_get_changelogs
added.description: Bulk fetch changelogs for Jira issues using /rest/api/3/changelog/bulkfetch.

added.name: jira_create_board
added.description: Create a Jira Software board. When filterId is omitted, this tool creates a Jira filter first using /rest/api/3/filter.

added.name: jira_create_issue

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

promptGuidelines: Use story_context first when the user provides a Jira key for implementation work.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

added.name: ctx_edit
added.description: Search-and-replace edit with race-condition guards. old_string must be unique unless replace_all=true. create=true writes new files. For editing code you've read, prefer ctx_patch (hash-anchored). Pi's native edit tool is always available — use ctx_edit when you need TOCTOU protection or lean-ctx cache coherence.

create.description: Create file if it does not exist

path.description: File path to edit

promptGuidelines: Pi's native edit is preferred for most edits (better TUI diff rendering).
Use ctx_edit when cache coherence matters or for race-condition protection.

added.name: ctx_find
added.description: Find files by glob. Prefer over native find/fd (gitignore-aware). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to reduce output size.

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

added.name: ctx_grep
added.description: Search code. Prefer over native Grep/ripgrep (compact, ranked). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to cap matches, context for surrounding lines.

context.description: Lines of context around each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

added.name: ctx_ls
added.description: List a directory. Prefer over native ls (compact, summarized). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to reduce output size.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

added.name: ctx_read
added.description: Read a file. Prefer over native Read/cat/head/tail (cached, compressed). Unchanged re-reads cost ~13 tokens. Auto-selects mode: configs (.yaml/.json/.toml/.env) are always full-read. Code files: full (<8KB), map (8-96KB), signatures (>96KB). Add mode=full to get complete file content. Use offset and limit to read specific line ranges.

fresh.description: Force a fresh disk re-read, bypassing the session cache

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

mode.description: Override auto-selection: full (complete content), map (deps+API signatures), signatures (AST only)

offset.description: Alias for start_line — 1-based line to start reading from

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

start_line.description: 1-based line to start reading from (alias: offset)

promptGuidelines: Use ctx_read to inspect file contents instead of cat or less.
Use mode=full if you need the complete file content.

added.name: ctx_shell
added.description: Run shell commands. Prefer over native Bash/shell (auto-compressed output). Runs the system POSIX shell ($SHELL, sh/bash-compatible) non-login, non-interactive and profile-free: no .bash_profile/.zshrc/rc files are sourced, so behavior is deterministic regardless of user shell config. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use ctx_shell to read files (cat/head/tail) — use ctx_read instead. Do NOT use ctx_shell for grep/find/ls — use ctx_grep, ctx_find, ctx_ls. Set raw=true to skip compression when exact output matters. Use timeout (seconds) to prevent hanging commands.

raw.description: Skip compression, return full uncompressed output

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds to prevent hanging commands

promptGuidelines: Use ctx_shell only for commands with side effects: build, test, install, git, run scripts.

added.name: lean_ctx
added.description: Run lean-ctx CLI directly (CLI-first; no MCP required). Use this for advanced commands like session/knowledge/overview/gain/stats/index/pack.

items.description: Arguments after 'lean-ctx'. Example: ['overview'] or ['knowledge','recall','Pi']

added.name: lean-ctx
added.description: Show lean-ctx status: binary path, MCP bridge, and registered tools

added.name: lean-ctx

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: ctx_edit
allTools.description: Search-and-replace edit with race-condition guards. old_string must be unique unless replace_all=true. create=true writes new files. For editing code you've read, prefer ctx_patch (hash-anchored). Pi's native edit tool is always available — use ctx_edit when you need TOCTOU protection or lean-ctx cache coherence.

create.description: Create file if it does not exist

path.description: File path to edit

promptGuidelines: Pi's native edit is preferred for most edits (better TUI diff rendering).
Use ctx_edit when cache coherence matters or for race-condition protection.

allTools.name: ctx_find
allTools.description: Find files by glob. Prefer over native find/fd (gitignore-aware). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to reduce output size.

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

allTools.name: ctx_grep
allTools.description: Search code. Prefer over native Grep/ripgrep (compact, ranked). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to cap matches, context for surrounding lines.

context.description: Lines of context around each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

allTools.name: ctx_ls
allTools.description: List a directory. Prefer over native ls (compact, summarized). `path` is required — pass the directory you are working in. Use limit to reduce output size.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list. Pass the directory you are working in — there is no cwd fallback.

allTools.name: ctx_read
allTools.description: Read a file. Prefer over native Read/cat/head/tail (cached, compressed). Unchanged re-reads cost ~13 tokens. Auto-selects mode: configs (.yaml/.json/.toml/.env) are always full-read. Code files: full (<8KB), map (8-96KB), signatures (>96KB). Add mode=full to get complete file content. Use offset and limit to read specific line ranges.

fresh.description: Force a fresh disk re-read, bypassing the session cache

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

mode.description: Override auto-selection: full (complete content), map (deps+API signatures), signatures (AST only)

offset.description: Alias for start_line — 1-based line to start reading from

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

start_line.description: 1-based line to start reading from (alias: offset)

promptGuidelines: Use ctx_read to inspect file contents instead of cat or less.
Use mode=full if you need the complete file content.

allTools.name: ctx_shell
allTools.description: Run shell commands. Prefer over native Bash/shell (auto-compressed output). Runs the system POSIX shell ($SHELL, sh/bash-compatible) non-login, non-interactive and profile-free: no .bash_profile/.zshrc/rc files are sourced, so behavior is deterministic regardless of user shell config. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use ctx_shell to read files (cat/head/tail) — use ctx_read instead. Do NOT use ctx_shell for grep/find/ls — use ctx_grep, ctx_find, ctx_ls. Set raw=true to skip compression when exact output matters. Use timeout (seconds) to prevent hanging commands.

raw.description: Skip compression, return full uncompressed output

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds to prevent hanging commands

promptGuidelines: Use ctx_shell only for commands with side effects: build, test, install, git, run scripts.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

added.name: shazam_changes
added.description:
Without this, you optimize the wrong files. Returns a concise summary
		of what changed in the working tree: changed files, affected symbols,
		risk level, and which callers may be impacted. Use after edits to see
		the blast radius before running full verification.

added.name: shazam_format
added.description:
When shazam_verify reports format or lint errors, use this to
		auto-fix them. Runs nearest-wins formatters (prettier, biome, eslint
		--fix, ruff, cargo fmt, gofmt). Format only - never touches logic.
		Always run with --dry-run first to preview changes before applying.

added.name: shazam_impact
added.description:
Required before editing 2+ files or any shared/exported module.
		Returns every file, symbol, and test affected by your planned changes.
		Without this, you are guessing which tests to run and which callers to
		update. Pass --with-symbols for per-symbol risk breakdown. Pass
		--compact for concise output (file names only). Pass --depth to
		control BFS traversal depth (default 3). Supports multiple --files.
		Pass --symbol for per-symbol caller/callee tracing (replaces
		shazam_call_chain). Pass --flat for a flat list of references.
		Pass --direction to filter by incoming/outgoing/both.

added.name: shazam_lookup
added.description:
Look up anything in the codebase - a symbol by name or a file by path.
		Auto-detects whether the input is a file path or symbol name and returns
			the most relevant information: definition, kind, signature, type hierarchy,
			file structure, PageRank, callers/callees. Use mode=search for fuzzy concept search, or just ask in natural language (auto-detected).
			Pass showCallbacks=true to expand anonymous functions.

added.name: shazam_overview
added.description:
When you first enter a project or return after changes - use this to
		understand the codebase before reading a single file. Returns: module
		dependency map, top-10 highest-PageRank files (the "spine"), key
		dependencies, recent git changes, entry points, reading order, HTTP
		route inventory, and complexity hotspots ranked by blast radius.
		Supports --filter to locate files by keyword.

		Output: plain text summary by default. Pass { json: true } for
		structured output with file lists and PageRank scores.

added.name: shazam_rename_symbol
added.description:
Required safety gate before renaming any symbol. Step 1: call
		shazam_impact --symbol to review all references. Step 2: use this to
		perform the project-wide rename via LSP textDocument/rename. Step 3:
		call shazam_verify to confirm no broken references. This is a WRITE
		operation - do not manually find-and-replace; missed references
		become bugs.

added.name: shazam_verify
added.description:
After every write or edit, run this to confirm no errors were
		introduced. Runs LSP diagnostics (type errors, warnings), then graph
		analysis (git diff, risk level, orphan detection).
		Verdict: PASS / WARN / FAIL. Use --quick for a fast git-change-only
		check (~2s). Use --lspOnly for diagnostics only. Use --preCommit for
		stricter thresholds. Relies on the incremental mtime scan -- the
		on-disk graph cache is reused, not bypassed.

added.name: shazam-doctor
added.description: Health check: tree-sitter grammars, LSP servers, cache integrity, recent diagnostics

added.description: Health check: tree-sitter grammars, LSP servers, cache integrity, recent diagnostics
