added.description: Health check: tree-sitter grammars, LSP servers, cache integrity, recent diagnostics

added.name: shazam-doctor
added.description: Health check: tree-sitter grammars, LSP servers, cache integrity, recent diagnostics

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: shazam_changes
allTools.description:
Without this, you optimize the wrong files. Returns a concise summary
		of what changed in the working tree: changed files, affected symbols,
		risk level, and which callers may be impacted. Use after edits to see
		the blast radius before running full verification.

allTools.name: shazam_format
allTools.description:
When shazam_verify reports format or lint errors, use this to
		auto-fix them. Runs nearest-wins formatters (prettier, biome, eslint
		--fix, ruff, cargo fmt, gofmt). Format only - never touches logic.
		Always run with --dry-run first to preview changes before applying.

allTools.name: shazam_impact
allTools.description:
Required before editing 2+ files or any shared/exported module.
		Returns every file, symbol, and test affected by your planned changes.
		Without this, you are guessing which tests to run and which callers to
		update. Pass --with-symbols for per-symbol risk breakdown. Pass
		--compact for concise output (file names only). Pass --depth to
		control BFS traversal depth (default 3). Supports multiple --files.
		Pass --symbol for per-symbol caller/callee tracing (replaces
		shazam_call_chain). Pass --flat for a flat list of references.
		Pass --direction to filter by incoming/outgoing/both.

allTools.name: shazam_lookup
allTools.description:
Look up anything in the codebase - a symbol by name or a file by path.
		Auto-detects whether the input is a file path or symbol name and returns
			the most relevant information: definition, kind, signature, type hierarchy,
			file structure, PageRank, callers/callees. Use mode=search for fuzzy concept search, or just ask in natural language (auto-detected).
			Pass showCallbacks=true to expand anonymous functions.

allTools.name: shazam_overview
allTools.description:
When you first enter a project or return after changes - use this to
		understand the codebase before reading a single file. Returns: module
		dependency map, top-10 highest-PageRank files (the "spine"), key
		dependencies, recent git changes, entry points, reading order, HTTP
		route inventory, and complexity hotspots ranked by blast radius.
		Supports --filter to locate files by keyword.

		Output: plain text summary by default. Pass { json: true } for
		structured output with file lists and PageRank scores.

allTools.name: shazam_rename_symbol
allTools.description:
Required safety gate before renaming any symbol. Step 1: call
		shazam_impact --symbol to review all references. Step 2: use this to
		perform the project-wide rename via LSP textDocument/rename. Step 3:
		call shazam_verify to confirm no broken references. This is a WRITE
		operation - do not manually find-and-replace; missed references
		become bugs.

allTools.name: shazam_verify
allTools.description:
After every write or edit, run this to confirm no errors were
		introduced. Runs LSP diagnostics (type errors, warnings), then graph
		analysis (git diff, risk level, orphan detection).
		Verdict: PASS / WARN / FAIL. Use --quick for a fast git-change-only
		check (~2s). Use --lspOnly for diagnostics only. Use --preCommit for
		stricter thresholds. Relies on the incremental mtime scan -- the
		on-disk graph cache is reused, not bypassed.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

performance.reason: requires stable successful probes in both runtimes

added.name: ask_intake
added.description: Ask the user a structured questionnaire (single or multi-select, free text) and optionally persist answers to the durable store, the Obsidian vault, or both.

header.description: Short label used in the progress indicator

description.description: Description shown below or beside the label

question.description: Full prompt shown to the user

promptGuidelines: Use ask_intake when you need structured answers from the user and want them persisted.
persist='ledger' appends a row to the JSONL collection; persist='vault' writes a markdown note under Agent/Inbox/.
Answers are returned keyed by the question string.

added.name: discover_schema
added.description: Infer the schema of an unconfigured .jsonl file by sampling its first N rows and register it as a collection.

file.description: Path to the .jsonl file (absolute or relative to the project root)

name.description: Collection name to register (defaults to the file basename)

sample.description: Number of rows to sample for schema inference (default: 100)

promptGuidelines: Use when you have a .jsonl file that is not yet mapped in .vault-mind/vault-mind.config.json.
The tool reads the first N rows, infers field names and JSON types, and writes a CollectionDef to the project config.

added.name: tombstone_entry
added.description: Mark a entry as deleted by replacing it with a tombstone record in the JSONL log.

entryId.description: ID of the entry to tombstone

promptGuidelines: Use to redact or remove an entry without breaking the append-only JSONL format.
The original entry is replaced by a tombstone record at the same line position.

added.name: vault_append
added.description: Append content to the end of an existing vault file.

content.description: Content to append

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_append to add lines to an existing note without reading it first.
The file must already exist.

added.name: vault_list
added.description: List files in the Obsidian vault with optional folder and glob filters.

folder.description: Optional subfolder to scope the listing

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

promptGuidelines: Use vault_list to enumerate notes and attachments in the vault.
Results are relative paths from the vault root.

added.name: vault_read
added.description: Read the contents of a file relative to the vault root.

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_read to inspect a specific note or file in the Obsidian vault.
Paths are relative to the configured default vault root.

added.name: vault_search
added.description: Full-text search across indexed vault notes in the default collection.

limit.description: Max results (default 10)

query.description: Keyword or phrase to search for

promptGuidelines: Use vault_search to find notes that match keywords or phrases.
Results include source paths from the indexed collection.

added.name: vault_tags
added.description: Extract and count tags from markdown frontmatter across the vault.
promptGuidelines: Use vault_tags to discover the tag vocabulary used in the vault.
Counts both frontmatter tags and inline #tags in markdown files.

added.name: vault_write
added.description: Create or overwrite a file inside the Obsidian vault.

content.description: Full content to write

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_write to create new notes or overwrite existing ones.
Parent directories are created automatically.

added.name: vm_append
added.description: Append to a collection. Autopilot dual-writes to JSONL + LanceDB with optional auto-sync to vault.

collection.description: Target collection name (call vm_describe if unsure)

entry.description: Field-key → value map matching the collection schema

vault.description: Specific vault to sync to (default: all configured)

promptGuidelines: Use autopilot for everyday notes — deduplicates automatically.
Use gated mode when the fact needs human review (queues in pending).
Use strict mode only for the most sensitive facts requiring explicit user confirmation.
Entry keys must match the collection schema. Call vm_describe first if unsure.
When the user says "Remember: [fact]", automatically use vm_append to store it.
Set sync="auto" to push substantial entries to configured vault(s).

added.name: vm_backlinks
added.description: Find Obsidian notes that link to a given note. Uses the Marksman Markdown LSP when available.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
note can be a relative vault path (e.g. notes/idea.md) or a note title.
Returns an empty list if the note is not found.

added.name: vm_broken_links
added.description: List broken [[wikilinks]] and relative Markdown links across the vault using Marksman workspace diagnostics.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
Returns file paths, link ranges, and diagnostic messages for each broken link.

added.name: vm_codegraph_impact
added.description: Report which code depends on a given symbol using the workspace codegraph index.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up (function, class, variable, type, etc.)

promptGuidelines: Requires a `.codegraph/` index at the project root (`codegraph init && codegraph index`).
Returns direct callers and the full downstream impact set for the named symbol.
Useful for assessing blast radius before modifying an exported function, class, or type.

added.name: vm_collection_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove vault-owned collection definitions.
promptGuidelines: Use to inspect or manage collection definitions after setup.
Collection paths must be vault-relative .jsonl files.
Do not delete a collection until all reported references are removed.

added.name: vm_configure
added.description: Read or update the pi-vault-mind config at runtime.
promptGuidelines: Use vm_configure(action="read") to inspect current config.
Use vm_configure(action="update", config={...}) to change schema, add collections, or modify vaults.

added.name: vm_describe
added.description: Introspect a named collection: schema, entry count, and sample entries.
promptGuidelines: Call before vm_append if you are unsure what fields the collection expects.

added.name: vm_export
added.description: Export a collection to JSON, CSV, or Markdown.
promptGuidelines: Use to share, archive, or import collection data elsewhere. Schema keys become column headers for CSV/Markdown.

added.name: vm_ingest
added.description: Ingest documents (URLs, HTML, PDFs) into the vaultMind. Converts to clean markdown via any2md, then returns the text for entity extraction and vm_append.

source.description: URL, file path, or raw text to ingest

promptGuidelines: Use to convert external documents into collection-ready markdown.
Accepts URLs (https://...), local file paths, or raw text blobs.
When source is a URL, calls any2md to fetch and convert to markdown.
When source is a file path, reads the file directly (use any2md via bash for PDFs/DOCX).
Returns the converted markdown — you then call vm_append for extracted facts.

added.name: vm_injector_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove injector definitions for existing collections.
promptGuidelines: Use only with an existing target collection.
Validate regular expressions before saving them.

added.name: vm_promote
added.description: Promote entries from one collection to another via the pending queue for human approval.

entryIds.description: List of entry IDs to promote

targetCollection.description: Collection where entries should be moved

promptGuidelines: Use when you find an insight in one collection that should be promoted to a more permanent one.
Stages entries in "pending" for human review. Does NOT append directly to target.
Provide a clear reason why these entries are worth promoting.

added.name: vm_query
added.description: Deterministic JSONL search by collection name. Use for exact lookups and filtered retrieval.

limit.description: Max results (default 50)

offset.description: Results to skip (default 0)

query.description: Free-text substring search across all text fields

promptGuidelines: Use when you know the collection name and need exact matches.
query does substring search across all text fields; filters does exact key-value matching.
If the collection is missing, run /vm setup first.

added.name: vm_related
added.description: Build a neighborhood graph around a note using backlinks and forward links from the Marksman Markdown LSP.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
entity can be a relative vault path or note title.
depth is clamped between 1 and 3 to keep results focused.

added.name: vm_search
added.description: mode='auto' (recommended) infers the strategy from the query. Search vault collections. Defaults to hybrid (vector+FTS rank-merge). Use mode='semantic' for vector-only, 'fts' for keyword, 'graph' for entity traversal, 'vault' for Obsidian-native title/path/tag search, 'full' for all merged.

limit.description: Max results (default 5)

query.description: Natural-language or keyword search query

sources.description: Sources to search: 'vault' (default, collections/index) and/or 'sessions' (Pi session .jsonl files). Merges hits from all listed sources.

promptGuidelines: Use vm_search as the default search tool.
mode='hybrid' (default) merges vector semantic and full-text results with vector ranked first.

mode='semantic' is vector-only; mode='fts' is keyword-only; mode='graph' traverses entity relations; mode='vault' searches the Obsidian vault's own title/path/tag index; mode='full' runs all backends and merges results.
For graph mode, pass entity (defaults to query) and depth (defaults to 1).

added.name: vm_stats
added.description: Dashboard of all collections: counts, sizes, pending queue size, and LanceDB health.
promptGuidelines: Call for a quick overview before writing or after a batch of appends.

added.name: vm_status
added.description: Show LanceDB status: data directory and tables.

added.name: vm_sync
added.description: Push matching entries to an Obsidian vault as markdown pages or canvas graph files.

limit.description: Max entries (default 20)

query.description: Free-text search to select entries to sync

vault.description: Vault name (defaults to "default"). Use "*" for all configured vaults.

promptGuidelines: Use to push agent knowledge to a human-readable Obsidian vault.
Omit vault to use the configured default vault. Use vault="*" for all configured vaults.
format="markdown" writes individual .md files. format="canvas" generates a .canvas graph file from entity relations.

added.name: intake
added.description: Run an intake questionnaire from a JSON spec file. Optionally write the result to --output.

added.name: skill:github-release-notes-file
added.description: Create GitHub releases with multi-line notes by writing to a file and using gh --notes-file

added.name: skill:obsidian-guided-test-session
added.description: Run structured manual test sessions in the ReturnVape Obsidian vault: attach debugger, guide user through steps, poll console/errors, collect Developer Toolbox screenshots, and produce a token-efficient session report.

added.name: skill:obsidian-interactive-test
added.description: Use during plugin testing — enables debug monitoring, takes screenshots (via CLI or Developer Toolbox), inspects DOM, tails console, checks functional state.

added.name: skill:obsidian-plugin-publish
added.description: Publishing the Vault Mind Obsidian plugin from the pi-vault-mind monorepo to the separate obsidian-vault-mind repo via BRAT

added.name: skill:pi-vault-mind-release
added.description: Release workflow for the pi-vault-mind monorepo: version bump, build, commit, GitHub releases, Obsidian plugin publish, BRAT test-vault install, npm publish

added.name: skill:pre-test-release
added.description: Use before handing off to the user for manual testing — runs full CI, updates docs/versions, publishes plugin, resets test vault, and alerts for npm OTP.

added.name: skill:vault-mind
added.description: Universal append-only JSONL collection with LanceDB semantic search, full-text search, graph relationships, and tiered HITL (strict, gated, autopilot). Use when tracking structured facts, decisions, requirements, research, or any append-only record across projects. Supports dynamic context injection into prompts. Install the pi-vault-mind extension first.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-broadcaster
added.description: The NotebookLM Controller agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:podcast or status:needs-podcast. Uses nlm CLI or MCP tools to generate audio overviews, study guides, and flashcards from vault sources.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-heavy-lifter
added.description: The Delegator agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:deep-analysis or @agent:refactor. Acts as a commander for external coding assistants (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to handle massive context windows or complex codebase refactoring.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-manager
added.description: The primary interactive agent (Synthesizer) for pi-vault-mind. Use for high-level planning, roadmap synthesis, and reviewing the output of subagents. Controls the overall narrative and final approval of knowledge content.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-miner
added.description: The Researcher agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:research or @agent:ingest. Responsible for knowledge ingestion, entity extraction, deduplication, and writing to the collections.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-setup
added.description: Setup and management assistant for pi-vault-mind (the passive Obsidian vault extension for pi). Use for first-time setup, configuration changes, troubleshooting, Obsidian plugin recommendations, upgrade paths, multi-vault management, and dependency auditing. Trigger phrases: "set up vault-mind", "configure obsidian", "install pi-vault-mind", "/vm setup", "watcher not working", "vault-mind troubleshooting", "add another vault", "upgrade pi-vault-mind".

added.name: vm_backlinks
added.description: Find Obsidian notes that link to a given note. Uses the Marksman Markdown LSP when available.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
note can be a relative vault path (e.g. notes/idea.md) or a note title.
Returns an empty list if the note is not found.

added.name: vm_broken_links
added.description: List broken [[wikilinks]] and relative Markdown links across the vault using Marksman workspace diagnostics.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
Returns file paths, link ranges, and diagnostic messages for each broken link.

added.name: vm_codegraph_impact
added.description: Report which code depends on a given symbol using the workspace codegraph index.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up (function, class, variable, type, etc.)

promptGuidelines: Requires a `.codegraph/` index at the project root (`codegraph init && codegraph index`).
Returns direct callers and the full downstream impact set for the named symbol.
Useful for assessing blast radius before modifying an exported function, class, or type.

added.name: vm_collection_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove vault-owned collection definitions.
promptGuidelines: Use to inspect or manage collection definitions after setup.
Collection paths must be vault-relative .jsonl files.
Do not delete a collection until all reported references are removed.

added.name: vm_configure
added.description: Read or update the pi-vault-mind config at runtime.
promptGuidelines: Use vm_configure(action="read") to inspect current config.
Use vm_configure(action="update", config={...}) to change schema, add collections, or modify vaults.

added.name: vm_describe
added.description: Introspect a named collection: schema, entry count, and sample entries.
promptGuidelines: Call before vm_append if you are unsure what fields the collection expects.

added.name: vm_export
added.description: Export a collection to JSON, CSV, or Markdown.
promptGuidelines: Use to share, archive, or import collection data elsewhere. Schema keys become column headers for CSV/Markdown.

added.name: vm_ingest
added.description: Ingest documents (URLs, HTML, PDFs) into the vaultMind. Converts to clean markdown via any2md, then returns the text for entity extraction and vm_append.

source.description: URL, file path, or raw text to ingest

promptGuidelines: Use to convert external documents into collection-ready markdown.
Accepts URLs (https://...), local file paths, or raw text blobs.
When source is a URL, calls any2md to fetch and convert to markdown.
When source is a file path, reads the file directly (use any2md via bash for PDFs/DOCX).
Returns the converted markdown — you then call vm_append for extracted facts.

added.name: vm_injector_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove injector definitions for existing collections.
promptGuidelines: Use only with an existing target collection.
Validate regular expressions before saving them.

added.name: vm_promote
added.description: Promote entries from one collection to another via the pending queue for human approval.

entryIds.description: List of entry IDs to promote

targetCollection.description: Collection where entries should be moved

promptGuidelines: Use when you find an insight in one collection that should be promoted to a more permanent one.
Stages entries in "pending" for human review. Does NOT append directly to target.
Provide a clear reason why these entries are worth promoting.

added.name: vm_query
added.description: Deterministic JSONL search by collection name. Use for exact lookups and filtered retrieval.

limit.description: Max results (default 50)

offset.description: Results to skip (default 0)

query.description: Free-text substring search across all text fields

promptGuidelines: Use when you know the collection name and need exact matches.
query does substring search across all text fields; filters does exact key-value matching.
If the collection is missing, run /vm setup first.

added.name: vm_related
added.description: Build a neighborhood graph around a note using backlinks and forward links from the Marksman Markdown LSP.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
entity can be a relative vault path or note title.
depth is clamped between 1 and 3 to keep results focused.

added.name: vm_search
added.description: mode='auto' (recommended) infers the strategy from the query. Search vault collections. Defaults to hybrid (vector+FTS rank-merge). Use mode='semantic' for vector-only, 'fts' for keyword, 'graph' for entity traversal, 'vault' for Obsidian-native title/path/tag search, 'full' for all merged.

limit.description: Max results (default 5)

query.description: Natural-language or keyword search query

sources.description: Sources to search: 'vault' (default, collections/index) and/or 'sessions' (Pi session .jsonl files). Merges hits from all listed sources.

promptGuidelines: Use vm_search as the default search tool.
mode='hybrid' (default) merges vector semantic and full-text results with vector ranked first.

mode='semantic' is vector-only; mode='fts' is keyword-only; mode='graph' traverses entity relations; mode='vault' searches the Obsidian vault's own title/path/tag index; mode='full' runs all backends and merges results.
For graph mode, pass entity (defaults to query) and depth (defaults to 1).

added.name: vm_stats
added.description: Dashboard of all collections: counts, sizes, pending queue size, and LanceDB health.
promptGuidelines: Call for a quick overview before writing or after a batch of appends.

added.name: vm_status
added.description: Show LanceDB status: data directory and tables.

added.name: vm_sync
added.description: Push matching entries to an Obsidian vault as markdown pages or canvas graph files.

limit.description: Max entries (default 20)

query.description: Free-text search to select entries to sync

vault.description: Vault name (defaults to "default"). Use "*" for all configured vaults.

promptGuidelines: Use to push agent knowledge to a human-readable Obsidian vault.
Omit vault to use the configured default vault. Use vault="*" for all configured vaults.
format="markdown" writes individual .md files. format="canvas" generates a .canvas graph file from entity relations.

added.name: intake
added.description: Run an intake questionnaire from a JSON spec file. Optionally write the result to --output.

added.name: skill:github-release-notes-file
added.description: Create GitHub releases with multi-line notes by writing to a file and using gh --notes-file

added.name: skill:obsidian-guided-test-session
added.description: Run structured manual test sessions in the ReturnVape Obsidian vault: attach debugger, guide user through steps, poll console/errors, collect Developer Toolbox screenshots, and produce a token-efficient session report.

added.name: skill:obsidian-interactive-test
added.description: Use during plugin testing — enables debug monitoring, takes screenshots (via CLI or Developer Toolbox), inspects DOM, tails console, checks functional state.

added.name: skill:obsidian-plugin-publish
added.description: Publishing the Vault Mind Obsidian plugin from the pi-vault-mind monorepo to the separate obsidian-vault-mind repo via BRAT

added.name: skill:pi-vault-mind-release
added.description: Release workflow for the pi-vault-mind monorepo: version bump, build, commit, GitHub releases, Obsidian plugin publish, BRAT test-vault install, npm publish

added.name: skill:pre-test-release
added.description: Use before handing off to the user for manual testing — runs full CI, updates docs/versions, publishes plugin, resets test vault, and alerts for npm OTP.

added.name: skill:vault-mind
added.description: Universal append-only JSONL collection with LanceDB semantic search, full-text search, graph relationships, and tiered HITL (strict, gated, autopilot). Use when tracking structured facts, decisions, requirements, research, or any append-only record across projects. Supports dynamic context injection into prompts. Install the pi-vault-mind extension first.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-broadcaster
added.description: The NotebookLM Controller agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:podcast or status:needs-podcast. Uses nlm CLI or MCP tools to generate audio overviews, study guides, and flashcards from vault sources.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-heavy-lifter
added.description: The Delegator agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:deep-analysis or @agent:refactor. Acts as a commander for external coding assistants (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to handle massive context windows or complex codebase refactoring.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-manager
added.description: The primary interactive agent (Synthesizer) for pi-vault-mind. Use for high-level planning, roadmap synthesis, and reviewing the output of subagents. Controls the overall narrative and final approval of knowledge content.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-miner
added.description: The Researcher agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:research or @agent:ingest. Responsible for knowledge ingestion, entity extraction, deduplication, and writing to the collections.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-setup
added.description: Setup and management assistant for pi-vault-mind (the passive Obsidian vault extension for pi). Use for first-time setup, configuration changes, troubleshooting, Obsidian plugin recommendations, upgrade paths, multi-vault management, and dependency auditing. Trigger phrases: "set up vault-mind", "configure obsidian", "install pi-vault-mind", "/vm setup", "watcher not working", "vault-mind troubleshooting", "add another vault", "upgrade pi-vault-mind".

added.name: ask_intake
added.description: Ask the user a structured questionnaire (single or multi-select, free text) and optionally persist answers to the durable store, the Obsidian vault, or both.

header.description: Short label used in the progress indicator

description.description: Description shown below or beside the label

question.description: Full prompt shown to the user

promptGuidelines: Use ask_intake when you need structured answers from the user and want them persisted.
persist='ledger' appends a row to the JSONL collection; persist='vault' writes a markdown note under Agent/Inbox/.

added.name: discover_schema

file.description: Path to the .jsonl file (absolute or relative to the project root)

name.description: Collection name to register (defaults to the file basename)

sample.description: Number of rows to sample for schema inference (default: 100)

promptGuidelines: Use when you have a .jsonl file that is not yet mapped in .vault-mind/vault-mind.config.json.
The tool reads the first N rows, infers field names and JSON types, and writes a CollectionDef to the project config.

added.name: tombstone_entry
added.description: Mark a entry as deleted by replacing it with a tombstone record in the JSONL log.

entryId.description: ID of the entry to tombstone

promptGuidelines: Use to redact or remove an entry without breaking the append-only JSONL format.
The original entry is replaced by a tombstone record at the same line position.

added.name: vault_append
added.description: Append content to the end of an existing vault file.

content.description: Content to append

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_append to add lines to an existing note without reading it first.
The file must already exist.

added.name: vault_list
added.description: List files in the Obsidian vault with optional folder and glob filters.

folder.description: Optional subfolder to scope the listing

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

promptGuidelines: Use vault_list to enumerate notes and attachments in the vault.

added.name: vault_read
added.description: Read the contents of a file relative to the vault root.

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_read to inspect a specific note or file in the Obsidian vault.

added.name: vault_search
added.description: Full-text search across indexed vault notes in the default collection.

limit.description: Max results (default 10)

query.description: Keyword or phrase to search for

promptGuidelines: Use vault_search to find notes that match keywords or phrases.

added.name: vault_tags
added.description: Extract and count tags from markdown frontmatter across the vault.
promptGuidelines: Use vault_tags to discover the tag vocabulary used in the vault.

added.name: vault_write
added.description: Create or overwrite a file inside the Obsidian vault.

content.description: Full content to write

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_write to create new notes or overwrite existing ones.
Parent directories are created automatically.

added.name: vm_append
added.description: Append to a collection. Autopilot dual-writes to JSONL + LanceDB with optional auto-sync to vault.

collection.description: Target collection name (call vm_describe if unsure)

entry.description: Field-key → value map matching the collection schema

vault.description: Specific vault to sync to (default: all configured)

promptGuidelines: Use autopilot for everyday notes — deduplicates automatically.
Use gated mode when the fact needs human review (queues in pending).
Use strict mode only for the most sensitive facts requiring explicit user confirmation.
Entry keys must match the collection schema. Call vm_describe first if unsure.
When the user says "Remember: [fact]", automatically use vm_append to store it.

persist='ledger' appends a row to the JSONL collection; persist='vault' writes a markdown note under Agent/Inbox/.
Answers are returned keyed by the question string.

added.name: discover_schema
added.description: Infer the schema of an unconfigured .jsonl file by sampling its first N rows and register it as a collection.

file.description: Path to the .jsonl file (absolute or relative to the project root)

name.description: Collection name to register (defaults to the file basename)

sample.description: Number of rows to sample for schema inference (default: 100)

promptGuidelines: Use when you have a .jsonl file that is not yet mapped in .vault-mind/vault-mind.config.json.
The tool reads the first N rows, infers field names and JSON types, and writes a CollectionDef to the project config.

added.name: tombstone_entry
added.description: Mark a entry as deleted by replacing it with a tombstone record in the JSONL log.

entryId.description: ID of the entry to tombstone

promptGuidelines: Use to redact or remove an entry without breaking the append-only JSONL format.
The original entry is replaced by a tombstone record at the same line position.

added.name: vault_append
added.description: Append content to the end of an existing vault file.

content.description: Content to append

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_append to add lines to an existing note without reading it first.
The file must already exist.

added.name: vault_list
added.description: List files in the Obsidian vault with optional folder and glob filters.

folder.description: Optional subfolder to scope the listing

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

promptGuidelines: Use vault_list to enumerate notes and attachments in the vault.
Results are relative paths from the vault root.

added.name: vault_read
added.description: Read the contents of a file relative to the vault root.

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_read to inspect a specific note or file in the Obsidian vault.
Paths are relative to the configured default vault root.

added.name: vault_search
added.description: Full-text search across indexed vault notes in the default collection.

limit.description: Max results (default 10)

query.description: Keyword or phrase to search for

promptGuidelines: Use vault_search to find notes that match keywords or phrases.
Results include source paths from the indexed collection.

added.name: vault_tags
added.description: Extract and count tags from markdown frontmatter across the vault.
promptGuidelines: Use vault_tags to discover the tag vocabulary used in the vault.
Counts both frontmatter tags and inline #tags in markdown files.

added.name: vault_write
added.description: Create or overwrite a file inside the Obsidian vault.

content.description: Full content to write

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_write to create new notes or overwrite existing ones.
Parent directories are created automatically.

added.name: vm_append
added.description: Append to a collection. Autopilot dual-writes to JSONL + LanceDB with optional auto-sync to vault.

collection.description: Target collection name (call vm_describe if unsure)

entry.description: Field-key → value map matching the collection schema

vault.description: Specific vault to sync to (default: all configured)

promptGuidelines: Use autopilot for everyday notes — deduplicates automatically.
Use gated mode when the fact needs human review (queues in pending).
Use strict mode only for the most sensitive facts requiring explicit user confirmation.
Entry keys must match the collection schema. Call vm_describe first if unsure.
When the user says "Remember: [fact]", automatically use vm_append to store it.
Set sync="auto" to push substantial entries to configured vault(s).

added.name: vm_backlinks
added.description: Find Obsidian notes that link to a given note. Uses the Marksman Markdown LSP when available.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
note can be a relative vault path (e.g. notes/idea.md) or a note title.
Returns an empty list if the note is not found.

added.name: vm_broken_links
added.description: List broken [[wikilinks]] and relative Markdown links across the vault using Marksman workspace diagnostics.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
Returns file paths, link ranges, and diagnostic messages for each broken link.

added.name: vm_codegraph_impact
added.description: Report which code depends on a given symbol using the workspace codegraph index.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up (function, class, variable, type, etc.)

promptGuidelines: Requires a `.codegraph/` index at the project root (`codegraph init && codegraph index`).
Returns direct callers and the full downstream impact set for the named symbol.
Useful for assessing blast radius before modifying an exported function, class, or type.

added.name: vm_collection_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove vault-owned collection definitions.
promptGuidelines: Use to inspect or manage collection definitions after setup.
Collection paths must be vault-relative .jsonl files.
Do not delete a collection until all reported references are removed.

added.name: vm_configure
added.description: Read or update the pi-vault-mind config at runtime.
promptGuidelines: Use vm_configure(action="read") to inspect current config.
Use vm_configure(action="update", config={...}) to change schema, add collections, or modify vaults.

added.name: vm_describe
added.description: Introspect a named collection: schema, entry count, and sample entries.
promptGuidelines: Call before vm_append if you are unsure what fields the collection expects.

added.name: vm_export
added.description: Export a collection to JSON, CSV, or Markdown.
promptGuidelines: Use to share, archive, or import collection data elsewhere. Schema keys become column headers for CSV/Markdown.

added.name: vm_ingest
added.description: Ingest documents (URLs, HTML, PDFs) into the vaultMind. Converts to clean markdown via any2md, then returns the text for entity extraction and vm_append.

source.description: URL, file path, or raw text to ingest

promptGuidelines: Use to convert external documents into collection-ready markdown.
Accepts URLs (https://...), local file paths, or raw text blobs.
When source is a URL, calls any2md to fetch and convert to markdown.
When source is a file path, reads the file directly (use any2md via bash for PDFs/DOCX).
Returns the converted markdown — you then call vm_append for extracted facts.

added.name: vm_injector_manage
added.description: List, create, update, or remove injector definitions for existing collections.
promptGuidelines: Use only with an existing target collection.
Validate regular expressions before saving them.

added.name: vm_promote
added.description: Promote entries from one collection to another via the pending queue for human approval.

entryIds.description: List of entry IDs to promote

targetCollection.description: Collection where entries should be moved

promptGuidelines: Use when you find an insight in one collection that should be promoted to a more permanent one.
Stages entries in "pending" for human review. Does NOT append directly to target.
Provide a clear reason why these entries are worth promoting.

added.name: vm_query
added.description: Deterministic JSONL search by collection name. Use for exact lookups and filtered retrieval.

limit.description: Max results (default 50)

offset.description: Results to skip (default 0)

query.description: Free-text substring search across all text fields

promptGuidelines: Use when you know the collection name and need exact matches.
query does substring search across all text fields; filters does exact key-value matching.
If the collection is missing, run /vm setup first.

added.name: vm_related
added.description: Build a neighborhood graph around a note using backlinks and forward links from the Marksman Markdown LSP.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
entity can be a relative vault path or note title.
depth is clamped between 1 and 3 to keep results focused.

added.name: vm_search
added.description: mode='auto' (recommended) infers the strategy from the query. Search vault collections. Defaults to hybrid (vector+FTS rank-merge). Use mode='semantic' for vector-only, 'fts' for keyword, 'graph' for entity traversal, 'vault' for Obsidian-native title/path/tag search, 'full' for all merged.

limit.description: Max results (default 5)

query.description: Natural-language or keyword search query

sources.description: Sources to search: 'vault' (default, collections/index) and/or 'sessions' (Pi session .jsonl files). Merges hits from all listed sources.

promptGuidelines: Use vm_search as the default search tool.
mode='hybrid' (default) merges vector semantic and full-text results with vector ranked first.

mode='semantic' is vector-only; mode='fts' is keyword-only; mode='graph' traverses entity relations; mode='vault' searches the Obsidian vault's own title/path/tag index; mode='full' runs all backends and merges results.
For graph mode, pass entity (defaults to query) and depth (defaults to 1).

added.name: vm_stats
added.description: Dashboard of all collections: counts, sizes, pending queue size, and LanceDB health.
promptGuidelines: Call for a quick overview before writing or after a batch of appends.

added.name: vm_status
added.description: Show LanceDB status: data directory and tables.

added.name: vm_sync
added.description: Push matching entries to an Obsidian vault as markdown pages or canvas graph files.

limit.description: Max entries (default 20)

query.description: Free-text search to select entries to sync

vault.description: Vault name (defaults to "default"). Use "*" for all configured vaults.

promptGuidelines: Use to push agent knowledge to a human-readable Obsidian vault.
Omit vault to use the configured default vault. Use vault="*" for all configured vaults.
format="markdown" writes individual .md files. format="canvas" generates a .canvas graph file from entity relations.

added.name: intake
added.description: Run an intake questionnaire from a JSON spec file. Optionally write the result to --output.

added.name: skill:github-release-notes-file
added.description: Create GitHub releases with multi-line notes by writing to a file and using gh --notes-file

added.name: skill:obsidian-guided-test-session
added.description: Run structured manual test sessions in the ReturnVape Obsidian vault: attach debugger, guide user through steps, poll console/errors, collect Developer Toolbox screenshots, and produce a token-efficient session report.

added.name: skill:obsidian-interactive-test
added.description: Use during plugin testing — enables debug monitoring, takes screenshots (via CLI or Developer Toolbox), inspects DOM, tails console, checks functional state.

added.name: skill:obsidian-plugin-publish
added.description: Publishing the Vault Mind Obsidian plugin from the pi-vault-mind monorepo to the separate obsidian-vault-mind repo via BRAT

added.name: skill:pi-vault-mind-release
added.description: Release workflow for the pi-vault-mind monorepo: version bump, build, commit, GitHub releases, Obsidian plugin publish, BRAT test-vault install, npm publish

added.name: skill:pre-test-release
added.description: Use before handing off to the user for manual testing — runs full CI, updates docs/versions, publishes plugin, resets test vault, and alerts for npm OTP.

added.name: skill:vault-mind
added.description: Universal append-only JSONL collection with LanceDB semantic search, full-text search, graph relationships, and tiered HITL (strict, gated, autopilot). Use when tracking structured facts, decisions, requirements, research, or any append-only record across projects. Supports dynamic context injection into prompts. Install the pi-vault-mind extension first.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-broadcaster
added.description: The NotebookLM Controller agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:podcast or status:needs-podcast. Uses nlm CLI or MCP tools to generate audio overviews, study guides, and flashcards from vault sources.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-heavy-lifter
added.description: The Delegator agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:deep-analysis or @agent:refactor. Acts as a commander for external coding assistants (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to handle massive context windows or complex codebase refactoring.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-manager
added.description: The primary interactive agent (Synthesizer) for pi-vault-mind. Use for high-level planning, roadmap synthesis, and reviewing the output of subagents. Controls the overall narrative and final approval of knowledge content.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-miner
added.description: The Researcher agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:research or @agent:ingest. Responsible for knowledge ingestion, entity extraction, deduplication, and writing to the collections.

added.name: skill:vault-mind-setup
added.description: Setup and management assistant for pi-vault-mind (the passive Obsidian vault extension for pi). Use for first-time setup, configuration changes, troubleshooting, Obsidian plugin recommendations, upgrade paths, multi-vault management, and dependency auditing. Trigger phrases: "set up vault-mind", "configure obsidian", "install pi-vault-mind", "/vm setup", "watcher not working", "vault-mind troubleshooting", "add another vault", "upgrade pi-vault-mind".

allTools.name: ask_intake
allTools.description: Ask the user a structured questionnaire (single or multi-select, free text) and optionally persist answers to the durable store, the Obsidian vault, or both.

header.description: Short label used in the progress indicator

description.description: Description shown below or beside the label

question.description: Full prompt shown to the user

promptGuidelines: Use ask_intake when you need structured answers from the user and want them persisted.
persist='ledger' appends a row to the JSONL collection; persist='vault' writes a markdown note under Agent/Inbox/.
Answers are returned keyed by the question string.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: discover_schema
allTools.description: Infer the schema of an unconfigured .jsonl file by sampling its first N rows and register it as a collection.

file.description: Path to the .jsonl file (absolute or relative to the project root)

name.description: Collection name to register (defaults to the file basename)

sample.description: Number of rows to sample for schema inference (default: 100)

promptGuidelines: Use when you have a .jsonl file that is not yet mapped in .vault-mind/vault-mind.config.json.
The tool reads the first N rows, infers field names and JSON types, and writes a CollectionDef to the project config.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.

allTools.name: tombstone_entry
allTools.description: Mark a entry as deleted by replacing it with a tombstone record in the JSONL log.

entryId.description: ID of the entry to tombstone

promptGuidelines: Use to redact or remove an entry without breaking the append-only JSONL format.
The original entry is replaced by a tombstone record at the same line position.

allTools.name: vault_append
allTools.description: Append content to the end of an existing vault file.

content.description: Content to append

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_append to add lines to an existing note without reading it first.
The file must already exist.

allTools.description: List files in the Obsidian vault with optional folder and glob filters.

folder.description: Optional subfolder to scope the listing

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

promptGuidelines: Use vault_list to enumerate notes and attachments in the vault.

allTools.description: Read the contents of a file relative to the vault root.

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_read to inspect a specific note or file in the Obsidian vault.

format="markdown" writes individual .md files. format="canvas" generates a .canvas graph file from entity relations.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

commands.name: __extension_matrix_probe
commands.description: Emit the extension compatibility probe

commands.name: intake
commands.description: Run an intake questionnaire from a JSON spec file. Optionally write the result to --output.

commands.name: llama
commands.description: Manage llama.cpp router models

commands.name: skill:github-release-notes-file
commands.description: Create GitHub releases with multi-line notes by writing to a file and using gh --notes-file

commands.name: skill:obsidian-guided-test-session
commands.description: Run structured manual test sessions in the ReturnVape Obsidian vault: attach debugger, guide user through steps, poll console/errors, collect Developer Toolbox screenshots, and produce a token-efficient session report.

commands.name: skill:obsidian-interactive-test
commands.description: Use during plugin testing — enables debug monitoring, takes screenshots (via CLI or Developer Toolbox), inspects DOM, tails console, checks functional state.

commands.name: skill:obsidian-plugin-publish
commands.description: Publishing the Vault Mind Obsidian plugin from the pi-vault-mind monorepo to the separate obsidian-vault-mind repo via BRAT

commands.name: skill:pi-vault-mind-release
commands.description: Release workflow for the pi-vault-mind monorepo: version bump, build, commit, GitHub releases, Obsidian plugin publish, BRAT test-vault install, npm publish

commands.name: skill:pre-test-release
commands.description: Use before handing off to the user for manual testing — runs full CI, updates docs/versions, publishes plugin, resets test vault, and alerts for npm OTP.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind
commands.description: Universal append-only JSONL collection with LanceDB semantic search, full-text search, graph relationships, and tiered HITL (strict, gated, autopilot). Use when tracking structured facts, decisions, requirements, research, or any append-only record across projects. Supports dynamic context injection into prompts. Install the pi-vault-mind extension first.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind-broadcaster
commands.description: The NotebookLM Controller agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:podcast or status:needs-podcast. Uses nlm CLI or MCP tools to generate audio overviews, study guides, and flashcards from vault sources.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind-heavy-lifter
commands.description: The Delegator agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:deep-analysis or @agent:refactor. Acts as a commander for external coding assistants (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to handle massive context windows or complex codebase refactoring.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind-manager
commands.description: The primary interactive agent (Synthesizer) for pi-vault-mind. Use for high-level planning, roadmap synthesis, and reviewing the output of subagents. Controls the overall narrative and final approval of knowledge content.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind-miner
commands.description: The Researcher agent for pi-vault-mind. Triggered by @agent:research or @agent:ingest. Responsible for knowledge ingestion, entity extraction, deduplication, and writing to the collections.

commands.name: skill:vault-mind-setup
commands.description: Setup and management assistant for pi-vault-mind (the passive Obsidian vault extension for pi). Use for first-time setup, configuration changes, troubleshooting, Obsidian plugin recommendations, upgrade paths, multi-vault management, and dependency auditing. Trigger phrases: "set up vault-mind", "configure obsidian", "install pi-vault-mind", "/vm setup", "watcher not working", "vault-mind troubleshooting", "add another vault", "upgrade pi-vault-mind".

commands.name: vm
commands.description: pi-vault-mind: manage collections, embedding, indexing, and config.

allTools.name: ask_intake
allTools.description: Ask the user a structured questionnaire (single or multi-select, free text) and optionally persist answers to the durable store, the Obsidian vault, or both.

header.description: Short label used in the progress indicator

description.description: Description shown below or beside the label

question.description: Full prompt shown to the user

promptGuidelines: Use ask_intake when you need structured answers from the user and want them persisted.
persist='ledger' appends a row to the JSONL collection; persist='vault' writes a markdown note under Agent/Inbox/.
Answers are returned keyed by the question string.

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: discover_schema
allTools.description: Infer the schema of an unconfigured .jsonl file by sampling its first N rows and register it as a collection.

file.description: Path to the .jsonl file (absolute or relative to the project root)

name.description: Collection name to register (defaults to the file basename)

sample.description: Number of rows to sample for schema inference (default: 100)

promptGuidelines: Use when you have a .jsonl file that is not yet mapped in .vault-mind/vault-mind.config.json.
The tool reads the first N rows, infers field names and JSON types, and writes a CollectionDef to the project config.

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

allTools.name: read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

allTools.name: tombstone_entry
allTools.description: Mark a entry as deleted by replacing it with a tombstone record in the JSONL log.

entryId.description: ID of the entry to tombstone

promptGuidelines: Use to redact or remove an entry without breaking the append-only JSONL format.
The original entry is replaced by a tombstone record at the same line position.

allTools.name: vault_append
allTools.description: Append content to the end of an existing vault file.

content.description: Content to append

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_append to add lines to an existing note without reading it first.
The file must already exist.

allTools.name: vault_list
allTools.description: List files in the Obsidian vault with optional folder and glob filters.

folder.description: Optional subfolder to scope the listing

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

promptGuidelines: Use vault_list to enumerate notes and attachments in the vault.
Results are relative paths from the vault root.

allTools.name: vault_read
allTools.description: Read the contents of a file relative to the vault root.

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_read to inspect a specific note or file in the Obsidian vault.
Paths are relative to the configured default vault root.

allTools.name: vault_search
allTools.description: Full-text search across indexed vault notes in the default collection.

limit.description: Max results (default 10)

query.description: Keyword or phrase to search for

promptGuidelines: Use vault_search to find notes that match keywords or phrases.
Results include source paths from the indexed collection.

allTools.name: vault_tags
allTools.description: Extract and count tags from markdown frontmatter across the vault.
promptGuidelines: Use vault_tags to discover the tag vocabulary used in the vault.
Counts both frontmatter tags and inline #tags in markdown files.

allTools.name: vault_write
allTools.description: Create or overwrite a file inside the Obsidian vault.

content.description: Full content to write

path.description: Relative path from the vault root

promptGuidelines: Use vault_write to create new notes or overwrite existing ones.
Parent directories are created automatically.

allTools.name: vm_append
allTools.description: Append to a collection. Autopilot dual-writes to JSONL + LanceDB with optional auto-sync to vault.

collection.description: Target collection name (call vm_describe if unsure)

entry.description: Field-key → value map matching the collection schema

vault.description: Specific vault to sync to (default: all configured)

promptGuidelines: Use autopilot for everyday notes — deduplicates automatically.
Use gated mode when the fact needs human review (queues in pending).
Use strict mode only for the most sensitive facts requiring explicit user confirmation.
Entry keys must match the collection schema. Call vm_describe first if unsure.
When the user says "Remember: [fact]", automatically use vm_append to store it.
Set sync="auto" to push substantial entries to configured vault(s).

allTools.name: vm_backlinks
allTools.description: Find Obsidian notes that link to a given note. Uses the Marksman Markdown LSP when available.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
note can be a relative vault path (e.g. notes/idea.md) or a note title.
Returns an empty list if the note is not found.

allTools.name: vm_broken_links
allTools.description: List broken [[wikilinks]] and relative Markdown links across the vault using Marksman workspace diagnostics.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
Returns file paths, link ranges, and diagnostic messages for each broken link.

allTools.name: vm_codegraph_impact
allTools.description: Report which code depends on a given symbol using the workspace codegraph index.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up (function, class, variable, type, etc.)

promptGuidelines: Requires a `.codegraph/` index at the project root (`codegraph init && codegraph index`).
Returns direct callers and the full downstream impact set for the named symbol.
Useful for assessing blast radius before modifying an exported function, class, or type.

allTools.name: vm_collection_manage
allTools.description: List, create, update, or remove vault-owned collection definitions.
promptGuidelines: Use to inspect or manage collection definitions after setup.
Collection paths must be vault-relative .jsonl files.
Do not delete a collection until all reported references are removed.

allTools.name: vm_configure
allTools.description: Read or update the pi-vault-mind config at runtime.
promptGuidelines: Use vm_configure(action="read") to inspect current config.
Use vm_configure(action="update", config={...}) to change schema, add collections, or modify vaults.

allTools.name: vm_describe
allTools.description: Introspect a named collection: schema, entry count, and sample entries.
promptGuidelines: Call before vm_append if you are unsure what fields the collection expects.

allTools.name: vm_export
allTools.description: Export a collection to JSON, CSV, or Markdown.
promptGuidelines: Use to share, archive, or import collection data elsewhere. Schema keys become column headers for CSV/Markdown.

allTools.name: vm_ingest
allTools.description: Ingest documents (URLs, HTML, PDFs) into the vaultMind. Converts to clean markdown via any2md, then returns the text for entity extraction and vm_append.

source.description: URL, file path, or raw text to ingest

promptGuidelines: Use to convert external documents into collection-ready markdown.
Accepts URLs (https://...), local file paths, or raw text blobs.
When source is a URL, calls any2md to fetch and convert to markdown.
When source is a file path, reads the file directly (use any2md via bash for PDFs/DOCX).
Returns the converted markdown — you then call vm_append for extracted facts.

allTools.name: vm_injector_manage
allTools.description: List, create, update, or remove injector definitions for existing collections.
promptGuidelines: Use only with an existing target collection.
Validate regular expressions before saving them.

allTools.name: vm_promote
allTools.description: Promote entries from one collection to another via the pending queue for human approval.

entryIds.description: List of entry IDs to promote

targetCollection.description: Collection where entries should be moved

promptGuidelines: Use when you find an insight in one collection that should be promoted to a more permanent one.
Stages entries in "pending" for human review. Does NOT append directly to target.
Provide a clear reason why these entries are worth promoting.

allTools.name: vm_query
allTools.description: Deterministic JSONL search by collection name. Use for exact lookups and filtered retrieval.

limit.description: Max results (default 50)

offset.description: Results to skip (default 0)

query.description: Free-text substring search across all text fields

promptGuidelines: Use when you know the collection name and need exact matches.
query does substring search across all text fields; filters does exact key-value matching.
If the collection is missing, run /vm setup first.

allTools.name: vm_related
allTools.description: Build a neighborhood graph around a note using backlinks and forward links from the Marksman Markdown LSP.
promptGuidelines: Requires Marksman (Markdown LSP) to be installed and on PATH.
entity can be a relative vault path or note title.
depth is clamped between 1 and 3 to keep results focused.

allTools.name: vm_search
allTools.description: mode='auto' (recommended) infers the strategy from the query. Search vault collections. Defaults to hybrid (vector+FTS rank-merge). Use mode='semantic' for vector-only, 'fts' for keyword, 'graph' for entity traversal, 'vault' for Obsidian-native title/path/tag search, 'full' for all merged.

limit.description: Max results (default 5)

query.description: Natural-language or keyword search query

sources.description: Sources to search: 'vault' (default, collections/index) and/or 'sessions' (Pi session .jsonl files). Merges hits from all listed sources.

promptGuidelines: Use vm_search as the default search tool.
mode='hybrid' (default) merges vector semantic and full-text results with vector ranked first.

mode='semantic' is vector-only; mode='fts' is keyword-only; mode='graph' traverses entity relations; mode='vault' searches the Obsidian vault's own title/path/tag index; mode='full' runs all backends and merges results.
For graph mode, pass entity (defaults to query) and depth (defaults to 1).

allTools.name: vm_stats
allTools.description: Dashboard of all collections: counts, sizes, pending queue size, and LanceDB health.
promptGuidelines: Call for a quick overview before writing or after a batch of appends.

allTools.name: vm_status
allTools.description: Show LanceDB status: data directory and tables.

allTools.name: vm_sync
allTools.description: Push matching entries to an Obsidian vault as markdown pages or canvas graph files.

limit.description: Max entries (default 20)

query.description: Free-text search to select entries to sync

vault.description: Vault name (defaults to "default"). Use "*" for all configured vaults.

promptGuidelines: Use to push agent knowledge to a human-readable Obsidian vault.
Omit vault to use the configured default vault. Use vault="*" for all configured vaults.
format="markdown" writes individual .md files. format="canvas" generates a .canvas graph file from entity relations.

allTools.name: write
allTools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)
