added.name: mem_capture_passive
added.description: Engram memory tool: capture passive. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

content.description: Text output containing a ## Key Learnings section

source.description: Source identifier, e.g. subagent-stop or session-end

added.name: mem_compare
added.description: Engram memory tool: compare. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

memory_id_a.description: Integer id of the first observation

memory_id_b.description: Integer id of the second observation

model.description: Model identifier for provenance

relation.description: Verdict: related | compatible | scoped | conflicts_with | supersedes | not_conflict

added.name: mem_context
added.description: Engram memory tool: context. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

scope.description: Filter observations by scope

added.name: mem_current_project
added.description: Engram memory tool: current project. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

cwd.description: Working directory to inspect; defaults to Engram server cwd

added.name: mem_delete
added.description: Engram memory tool: delete. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

added.name: mem_doctor
added.description: Engram memory tool: doctor. Compact UI is provided by gentle-engram; persistence is handled by Engram when installed and running.

check.description: Optional diagnostic check code to run

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

added.name: read
added.description:
Read a text file. Each line is returned as `HASH│content`.

Key rule: line numbers are NOT part of the output. Use the 3-char HASH to reference lines in replace calls. A HASH may change when the line content changes.

HASH format:
- The HASH is 3 characters from the URL-safe base64 alphabet `A-Za-z0-9-_` (e.g. `aB3`, `4yN`, `-qk`).
- The content after the `│` separator is the line verbatim.
- The line number is not part of the output. Use the HASH to reference lines.

Pagination:
- Large files return a truncated preview with a pagination hint (e.g. `[Showing lines 1-100 of 500. Use offset=101 to continue.]`). Call `read` again with `offset=N` to continue.
- Default cap: 2000 lines or 50.0KB; output exceeding either is truncated. Pass `limit` to read fewer lines.

File kinds:
- Text files are returned as `HASH│content` lines.
- Images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP) are returned as visual attachments.
- Binary files and directories are rejected with a descriptive error.
- Empty files are returned as a single empty-line hash (`HASH│`). Use replace on that hash to insert content.

Non-UTF-8 bytes:
- UTF-8 byte-order marks (BOM) are stripped. Editing a file with a BOM rewrites it without the BOM.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use `read` before `replace` when you do not have current HASH anchors for the file.
A HASH may contain `-` as a normal alphabet character.

added.name: replace
added.description:
Replace lines in a text file using HASH anchors from `read`.

Put all operations on one file in a single `replace` call. Stack every region into the `changes` array, even when they are far apart. Anchors within one call must all come from the same pre-edit read; the runtime applies them atomically against that one snapshot.

Workflow:
1. Call `read` to get `HASH│content` lines for the file.
2. Identify the range to replace using the 3-char hashes from the read output.
3. Call `replace` with `hash_range_inclusive` (the two hashes) and `content_lines` (the new lines).
4. On `[E_STALE_ANCHOR]`, call `read` again for fresh anchors, then retry.
5. On success, call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Request structure:
Bulk mode (default):
```json
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": [...], "hash_range_inclusive": ["aB3", "xY7"] }], "path": "..." }
```

Fields:
- content_lines — replacement lines as a JSON array of strings. File content only — no HASH│ prefix.
- hash_range_inclusive — [start_hash, end_hash] from read output. 3-char base64 hash only — no │ or line content.
- path — file to edit.

Examples:

Single line:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["const x = 1;"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["MQX", "MQX"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Range replace:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["function greet() {", "  return 1;", "}"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["ZPM", "VRW"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Rules:
- Anchors must be 3-char base64 hashes from the most recent read. Stale anchors fail with [E_STALE_ANCHOR].
- The range is inclusive: every line from start_hash through end_hash is deleted.
- Those lines are replaced with content_lines — nothing is inserted, nothing is appended.
- content_lines is literal file content. Never include the HASH│ prefix — that goes in hash_range_inclusive.
- content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a serialized string.
- Preserve leading whitespace exactly as it appears after │ in read output.
- To delete lines, use content_lines: [].
- If content_lines matches current content, the edit is a noop (file unchanged).
- Multiple edits in one call must not overlap. Overlapping ranges are rejected with [E_EDIT_CONFLICT].
On success, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Recovery: If a replace produces incorrect results, call undo_last_replace with the file path to revert.

content_lines.description: literal replacement file content, one string per line. Must not include the HASH│ prefix from read output.

hash_range_inclusive.description: inclusive hash range to replace [start_hash, end_hash]. Each element must be the 3-character hash anchor only; do not include the │ separator or line content.

items.description: anchor (3-char HASH)

path.description: path

promptGuidelines: Use `replace` with HASH anchors for all file changes; batch every change to one file into a single `replace` call.
After a successful replace, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.
On [E_STALE_ANCHOR], call read to get fresh anchors, copy the 3-character HASH of the start and end of the range into hash_range_inclusive, and retry.

hash_range_inclusive replaces the ENTIRE range inclusively. Every line from the first anchor through the second anchor is deleted. Only put replacement lines in content_lines — do not include lines that already exist outside the range.
Preserve leading whitespace exactly. The content after │ in read output includes all leading spaces and tabs — copy them into content_lines unchanged.
content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a JSON string.

added.name: undo_last_replace
added.description: Undo the last replace operation on a file, reverting it to its previous state. Use this when a replace produced incorrect results (e.g., wrong content, duplicated lines, broken syntax). After undoing, call `read` to get fresh anchors for a corrected replace.

path.description: Path to the file to undo the last replace on

promptGuidelines: Call `undo_last_replace` with the file path to revert the last replace on that file.
After undoing, call `read` to get fresh anchors for a corrected replace.
Only the most recent replace per file is tracked — calling undo twice without an intervening replace will produce "no undo history".

added.name: read
added.description:
Read a text file. Each line is returned as `HASH│content`.

Key rule: line numbers are NOT part of the output. Use the 3-char HASH to reference lines in replace calls. A HASH may change when the line content changes.

HASH format:
- The HASH is 3 characters from the URL-safe base64 alphabet `A-Za-z0-9-_` (e.g. `aB3`, `4yN`, `-qk`).
- The content after the `│` separator is the line verbatim.
- The line number is not part of the output. Use the HASH to reference lines.

Pagination:
- Large files return a truncated preview with a pagination hint (e.g. `[Showing lines 1-100 of 500. Use offset=101 to continue.]`). Call `read` again with `offset=N` to continue.
- Default cap: 2000 lines or 50.0KB; output exceeding either is truncated. Pass `limit` to read fewer lines.

File kinds:
- Text files are returned as `HASH│content` lines.
- Images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP) are returned as visual attachments.
- Binary files and directories are rejected with a descriptive error.
- Empty files are returned as a single empty-line hash (`HASH│`). Use replace on that hash to insert content.

Non-UTF-8 bytes:
- UTF-8 byte-order marks (BOM) are stripped. Editing a file with a BOM rewrites it without the BOM.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use `read` before `replace` when you do not have current HASH anchors for the file.
A HASH may contain `-` as a normal alphabet character.

added.name: replace
added.description:
Replace lines in a text file using HASH anchors from `read`.

Put all operations on one file in a single `replace` call. Stack every region into the `changes` array, even when they are far apart. Anchors within one call must all come from the same pre-edit read; the runtime applies them atomically against that one snapshot.

Workflow:
1. Call `read` to get `HASH│content` lines for the file.
2. Identify the range to replace using the 3-char hashes from the read output.
3. Call `replace` with `hash_range_inclusive` (the two hashes) and `content_lines` (the new lines).
4. On `[E_STALE_ANCHOR]`, call `read` again for fresh anchors, then retry.
5. On success, call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Request structure:
Bulk mode (default):
```json
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": [...], "hash_range_inclusive": ["aB3", "xY7"] }], "path": "..." }
```

Fields:
- content_lines — replacement lines as a JSON array of strings. File content only — no HASH│ prefix.
- hash_range_inclusive — [start_hash, end_hash] from read output. 3-char base64 hash only — no │ or line content.
- path — file to edit.

Examples:

Single line:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["const x = 1;"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["MQX", "MQX"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Range replace:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["function greet() {", "  return 1;", "}"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["ZPM", "VRW"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Rules:
- Anchors must be 3-char base64 hashes from the most recent read. Stale anchors fail with [E_STALE_ANCHOR].
- The range is inclusive: every line from start_hash through end_hash is deleted.
- Those lines are replaced with content_lines — nothing is inserted, nothing is appended.
- content_lines is literal file content. Never include the HASH│ prefix — that goes in hash_range_inclusive.
- content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a serialized string.
- Preserve leading whitespace exactly as it appears after │ in read output.
- To delete lines, use content_lines: [].
- If content_lines matches current content, the edit is a noop (file unchanged).
- Multiple edits in one call must not overlap. Overlapping ranges are rejected with [E_EDIT_CONFLICT].
On success, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Recovery: If a replace produces incorrect results, call undo_last_replace with the file path to revert.

hash_range_inclusive.description: inclusive hash range to replace [start_hash, end_hash]. Each element must be the 3-character hash anchor only; do not include the │ separator or line content.

items.description: anchor (3-char HASH)

path.description: path

allTools.name: bash
allTools.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

allTools.name: edit
allTools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.

allTools.name: find
allTools.description: Search for files by glob pattern. Returns matching file paths relative to the search directory. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 1000 results or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 1000)

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.ts', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*.spec.ts'

allTools.name: grep
allTools.description: Search file contents for a pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Respects .gitignore. Output is truncated to 100 matches or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Long lines are truncated to 500 chars.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match (default: 0)

glob.description: Filter files by glob pattern, e.g. '*.ts' or '**/*.spec.ts'

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal string instead of regex (default: false)

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: current directory)

allTools.name: ls
allTools.description: List directory contents. Returns entries sorted alphabetically, with '/' suffix for directories. Includes dotfiles. Output is truncated to 500 entries or 50KB (whichever is hit first).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500)

path.description: Directory to list (default: current directory)

Examples:

Single line:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["const x = 1;"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["MQX", "MQX"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Range replace:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["function greet() {", "  return 1;", "}"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["ZPM", "VRW"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Rules:
- Anchors must be 3-char base64 hashes from the most recent read. Stale anchors fail with [E_STALE_ANCHOR].
- The range is inclusive: every line from start_hash through end_hash is deleted.
- Those lines are replaced with content_lines — nothing is inserted, nothing is appended.
- content_lines is literal file content. Never include the HASH│ prefix — that goes in hash_range_inclusive.
- content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a serialized string.
- Preserve leading whitespace exactly as it appears after │ in read output.
- To delete lines, use content_lines: [].
- If content_lines matches current content, the edit is a noop (file unchanged).
- Multiple edits in one call must not overlap. Overlapping ranges are rejected with [E_EDIT_CONFLICT].
On success, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Recovery: If a replace produces incorrect results, call undo_last_replace with the file path to revert.

content_lines.description: literal replacement file content, one string per line. Must not include the HASH│ prefix from read output.

hash_range_inclusive.description: inclusive hash range to replace [start_hash, end_hash]. Each element must be the 3-character hash anchor only; do not include the │ separator or line content.

items.description: anchor (3-char HASH)

path.description: path

promptGuidelines: Use `replace` with HASH anchors for all file changes; batch every change to one file into a single `replace` call.
After a successful replace, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.
On [E_STALE_ANCHOR], call read to get fresh anchors, copy the 3-character HASH of the start and end of the range into hash_range_inclusive, and retry.

hash_range_inclusive replaces the ENTIRE range inclusively. Every line from the first anchor through the second anchor is deleted. Only put replacement lines in content_lines — do not include lines that already exist outside the range.
Preserve leading whitespace exactly. The content after │ in read output includes all leading spaces and tabs — copy them into content_lines unchanged.
content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a JSON string.

allTools.name: undo_last_replace
allTools.description: Undo the last replace operation on a file, reverting it to its previous state. Use this when a replace produced incorrect results (e.g., wrong content, duplicated lines, broken syntax). After undoing, call `read` to get fresh anchors for a corrected replace.

path.description: Path to the file to undo the last replace on

promptGuidelines: Call `undo_last_replace` with the file path to revert the last replace on that file.
After undoing, call `read` to get fresh anchors for a corrected replace.
Only the most recent replace per file is tracked — calling undo twice without an intervening replace will produce "no undo history".

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.

allTools.name: read
allTools.description:
Read a text file. Each line is returned as `HASH│content`.

Key rule: line numbers are NOT part of the output. Use the 3-char HASH to reference lines in replace calls. A HASH may change when the line content changes.

HASH format:
- The HASH is 3 characters from the URL-safe base64 alphabet `A-Za-z0-9-_` (e.g. `aB3`, `4yN`, `-qk`).
- The content after the `│` separator is the line verbatim.
- The line number is not part of the output. Use the HASH to reference lines.

Pagination:
- Large files return a truncated preview with a pagination hint (e.g. `[Showing lines 1-100 of 500. Use offset=101 to continue.]`). Call `read` again with `offset=N` to continue.
- Default cap: 2000 lines or 50.0KB; output exceeding either is truncated. Pass `limit` to read fewer lines.

File kinds:
- Text files are returned as `HASH│content` lines.
- Images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP) are returned as visual attachments.
- Binary files and directories are rejected with a descriptive error.
- Empty files are returned as a single empty-line hash (`HASH│`). Use replace on that hash to insert content.

Non-UTF-8 bytes:
- UTF-8 byte-order marks (BOM) are stripped. Editing a file with a BOM rewrites it without the BOM.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

promptGuidelines: Use `read` before `replace` when you do not have current HASH anchors for the file.
A HASH may contain `-` as a normal alphabet character.

allTools.name: replace
allTools.description:
Replace lines in a text file using HASH anchors from `read`.

Put all operations on one file in a single `replace` call. Stack every region into the `changes` array, even when they are far apart. Anchors within one call must all come from the same pre-edit read; the runtime applies them atomically against that one snapshot.

Workflow:
1. Call `read` to get `HASH│content` lines for the file.
2. Identify the range to replace using the 3-char hashes from the read output.
3. Call `replace` with `hash_range_inclusive` (the two hashes) and `content_lines` (the new lines).
4. On `[E_STALE_ANCHOR]`, call `read` again for fresh anchors, then retry.
5. On success, call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Request structure:
Bulk mode (default):
```json
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": [...], "hash_range_inclusive": ["aB3", "xY7"] }], "path": "..." }
```

Fields:
- content_lines — replacement lines as a JSON array of strings. File content only — no HASH│ prefix.
- hash_range_inclusive — [start_hash, end_hash] from read output. 3-char base64 hash only — no │ or line content.
- path — file to edit.

Examples:

Single line:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["const x = 1;"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["MQX", "MQX"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Range replace:
{ "changes": [{ "content_lines": ["function greet() {", "  return 1;", "}"], "hash_range_inclusive": ["ZPM", "VRW"] }], "path": "src/main.ts" }

Rules:
- Anchors must be 3-char base64 hashes from the most recent read. Stale anchors fail with [E_STALE_ANCHOR].
- The range is inclusive: every line from start_hash through end_hash is deleted.
- Those lines are replaced with content_lines — nothing is inserted, nothing is appended.
- content_lines is literal file content. Never include the HASH│ prefix — that goes in hash_range_inclusive.
- content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a serialized string.
- Preserve leading whitespace exactly as it appears after │ in read output.
- To delete lines, use content_lines: [].
- If content_lines matches current content, the edit is a noop (file unchanged).
- Multiple edits in one call must not overlap. Overlapping ranges are rejected with [E_EDIT_CONFLICT].
On success, the response shows the change summary. Call `read` to get fresh anchors for follow-up edits.

Recovery: If a replace produces incorrect results, call undo_last_replace with the file path to revert.

content_lines.description: literal replacement file content, one string per line. Must not include the HASH│ prefix from read output.

hash_range_inclusive.description: inclusive hash range to replace [start_hash, end_hash]. Each element must be the 3-character hash anchor only; do not include the │ separator or line content.

items.description: anchor (3-char HASH)

path.description: path

promptGuidelines: Use `replace` with HASH anchors for all file changes; batch every change to one file into a single `replace` call.

On [E_STALE_ANCHOR], call read to get fresh anchors, copy the 3-character HASH of the start and end of the range into hash_range_inclusive, and retry.

hash_range_inclusive replaces the ENTIRE range inclusively. Every line from the first anchor through the second anchor is deleted. Only put replacement lines in content_lines — do not include lines that already exist outside the range.
Preserve leading whitespace exactly. The content after │ in read output includes all leading spaces and tabs — copy them into content_lines unchanged.
content_lines must be a native JSON array of strings, not a JSON string.
